programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy Lieutenant who has been murdered while on his way to a meeting with the President, and must ascertain whether the killing was part of a recent spate of muggings in the area - or a targeted hit to keep information secret. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky works on a case involving his estranged childhood best friend, travelling to London with Special Agent Bishop to interview his pal's family and co-workers. While away, the detective reflects on his own past choices, and examines how they have affected his present life. Alice Krige guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. The trail appears to lead to a friend of Sofia's, another scientist who worked for Russia and who may have succumbed to professional jealousy. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on a joint Coast Guard and NCIS training exercise at sea with Agent Borin, Gibbs spots a dead man overboard, who is found to be a missing member of the crew of a nearby vessel. However, when the agent boards the ship for questioning, he realises it has been commandeered by pirates. Naval drama, starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy commander's wife, determining whether it is linked to her role as a therapist, or an act of terrorism since her husband's name has appeared on a Jihadist's target list. Elsewhere, the group discusses Halloween costumes, as well as Tony's recent dating habits. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: Part two of two. The teams fly to Los Angeles from Hawaii in pursuit of suspect Jarrod Taylor, before tracking him down at his hotel, where he claims he is being paid by an immunologist who is hoping to reintroduce smallpox to restore nature's balance. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. The agents investigate when two men are killed at a gun shop, but soon discover one of their colleagues could be at risk. programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed by a stolen surveillance drone and the team links it to elusive terrorist Benham Parsa. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Smithy upsets the Longmarsh heavies by spurning their offer of protection and is beaten up for his pains, but it is the approach of Louise's funeral that drives him to despair. Dan Casper enters a supposedly empty flat where he is surprised to find a naked woman, and her confused explanation of how she came to be there finally gives the Sun Hill detectives a lead in the Pete Larson case. Bill Treacher guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Smithy is distraught when he's refused permission to attend Louise's funeral and loses control when he finds fellow prisoner Marty Johnson setting fire to a photograph of her. Jack approaches the CPS with evidence of the link between Pete Larson and Carl Caplan, but Keane will only grant CID 24 hours to put the suspect under surveillance and come up with concrete proof that the sergeant was framed

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Feelings are running high following the death of Jordan Tomlin and Okaro drafts in a full team to police a sell-out gig at the Bombastic nightclub. Forged tickets swell the crowd to dangerous levels and the punters turn nasty when Emma Keane is forced to arrest a man for possession of drugs. Faced with a potential riot outside, Yvonne Hemmingway makes a bad call which leads to disaster

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The aftermath of the nightclub disaster leaves the Sun Hill relief in shock and Adam Okaro summons all the self-control he can muster to set aside personal tragedy before meeting Met Press Officer Mia Perry. Will Fletcher and Tony Stamp face a traumatic morning trying to identify the bodies in a temporary morgue, and Emma Keane reunites a mother with her dying son

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew returns to Cardale after six months away and asks for his old job back. The others are furious at being given the runaround, having endured months of working with below-par locums, and consider appointing a rival candidate. On the personal front, Will is over the moon at becoming a father _ but there's a dark cloud on the horizon. Rural medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Saskia Wickham, Gary Mavers and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Secret
programme.desc: Feature-length dramatisation of the writer's only thriller, set in 19th-century Tyneside. Colin Buchanan stars as successful businessman Freddie Musgrave, who has worked hard for 20 years to live down his misspent youth as runner to a gang of smugglers and is shocked when a face appears from long ago, threatening to reveal the details of his sordid past. With Clare Higgins and June Whitfield

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A rare disease spreads around Poplar Street, and Patsy and Dr Turner try to trace the outbreak to its source. Suspicion over the identity of the first carrier threatens to divide a previously close-knit family. Sister Winifred is forced to confront her prejudices while treating a patient, and Barbara worries about how Trixie will react when she agrees to go to dinner with Tom

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is seconded to a maternity ward, and is impressed by how modern technology is being used to help the mothers - but is left with a moral dilemma after assisting on a traumatic birth. A young man from Poplar is delighted to be accepted into university, but his mother is less than happy. His dreams are jeopardised when his girlfriend gets pregnant, a development that puts pressure on the whole family

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard is forced to think on his feet when he forgets his wedding anniversary and tells Hyacinth he has arranged to have a security alarm fitted as a present. Social-climbing comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth plans an extravagant riverside picnic and invites all and sundry from the higher echelons of society, including the vicar and his wife. Alas for her dreams of social success, the event proves a wash-out. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth disappoints Richard by buying him a pair of skis for his birthday, intending to strap them to the car in yet another attempt to impress the neighbours. However, the scheme backfires when her rich sister Violet arrives and cuts her down to size

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Michelle Collins guest stars as a company boss who brings her staff to Saint Marie on an outdoor team-building exercise - although she isn't around for long as she gets bumped off in her shack. Humphrey tries to work out who is responsible - the aggrieved assistant, the long-lost son, the sister whose husband the victim slept with - or someone else. All is going well, until a shock confession brings the investigation to an abrupt halt - and an unexpected phone call from home only adds to the inspector's problems. With Kaye Wragg, Jason Merrells, Sylvestra Le Touzel, Matthew Lewis and Gary Lewis

Crime thriller, starring John Hannah and Eilidh MacDonald

programme.desc: The NLF want to make a trade. Alison watches over Ed, feeling conflicted over their marriage. Yusra smuggles a coded message to Armstrong, and Joe goes against his orders

programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when a fellow hotel guest is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Smithy gets a warm welcome when he reports for duty at Sun Hill, but is taken aback to discover he's assigned to investigate his former cellmate Morris Leatherhead. Single dad Neil Manson has to tell James Tennant the search for his daughter is to be scaled down, and learns how painful that must feel when his own son is reported missing from school. Suzie makes her disappointment clear

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant organises a drag act at the Vic. Michelle interviews Sharon for Geoff's book about her life in the East End, unaware certain revelations are on tape

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Michelle has a terrible hangover. David tries to make friends with Bianca. Carol and Alan are still hardly speaking, and Cindy goes swimming - again bumping into lifeguard Matt

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: George rescues a stewardess trapped in a smoke filled plane during a training exercise, and Joe's brother is caught up in an arson attack

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew is involved in a nasty car crash, but refuses to give in to his own injuries and, bloody but unbowed, crawls from the wreckage and begins the grim task of ensuring his fellow motorists make it through the night alive. Unaware of his partner's ordeal, David throws a surprise birthday party for Erica _ but things don't go according to plan. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers, Saskia Wickham and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: The doctors discover a bogus GP has been making house calls to Paul's patients under his name and call in police to investigate. The medic worries his reputation could be tarnished permanently - especially as the imposter seems to know his every move. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The success of a new business venture causes Sharon and Tracey's lives to take a new direction. Meanwhile, Dorien advises a depressed Garth, who's desperate to find out what life's all about. With Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana decide they would like a holiday, but face the task of upsetting the devious Harvey's construction plans to raise the necessary cash. Comedy, with Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The Resistance provides a waitress to fill the vacancy at Cafe Rene, and the Colonel is joined by an Italian liaison officer. Wartime comedy, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The friends meet a mysterious man who has a machine that can locate 'hidden forces'

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. At first Humphrey turns his attention to the small group he was with the night before he died - but as the investigation deepens, the investigation takes an unexpected turn that requires the team to leave the comfort of their Caribbean island - and go searching for their suspects in London. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Green-fingered amateur detectives Laura and Rosemary are called in to tackle a stubborn species of weed threatening an entire year's vintage at the Crickle Valley vineyard. However, they find themselves in the right place at the right time when the body of a wine critic is discovered on the premises. Murder mystery, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Standing and the team reinvestigate the 50-year-old murder of Frederick Tully, a wages clerk at Battersea Power Station. Discovering new evidence that leads them to doubt the man executed for the crime was guilty, they question the validity of the original case against him. Drama, guest starring George Cole, Roy Hudd and Jan Francis

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: An ageing confectionery company owner is found dead in his London hotel suite, and while Nikki tries to convince the sceptical DS Gold he was murdered, several businessmen begin to circle as it turns out the dead man was losing a desperate fight to prevent the sale of his company to investors. Feature-length episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and David Caves, with guest appearances by Tim Pigott-Smith, Sharon Small and Priyanga Burford

programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Ciara McEleney wants to move her whole family to Australia for the better weather. Husband Ruairi, however, is proving to be a cloud on her new horizons. A week in Perth sees them check out the forecast for moving Down Under on a permanent basis. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A meadow in Woodville in Derbyshire, an old mill in Stoke in Staffordshire and a terraced house in Mountain Ash in Wales are all sold under the hammer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall help teams look for items at the Mona Showground in Anglesey, while presenter Anita Manning finds out about Portmeirion pottery

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits London's Olympic Stadium to explore the links between Christianity and sport, and JB Gill meets Watford goalkeeper Heurelho Gomes, who is training to be a pastor

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an expat couple who want to return from Cyprus to buy a house in Lincolnshire, and also visits a college to find out about the next generation of farmers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Wallyford Recycling Centre in Scotland. Rupert Blanchard works on wooden garden chairs, while Bruce Faulseit turns a sewing machine stand into something special

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create a Scouts-inspired garden, complete with campfire and winding path, for Thomas and Kate in Harborne, Birmingham

programme.title: War Horse (2011)
programme.desc: A farmer's son forms a close bond with a horse he trains, but is forced to give the animal up when it is sold to the British army during the First World War. The horse passes through various owners on both sides as he travels across the battlefields of France, while the boy refuses to give up on being reunited. Steven Spielberg's drama based on Michael Morpurgo's book, starring Jeremy Irvine, Tom Hiddleston and Benedict Cumberbatch

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are at Ockham and Wisley Commons in Surrey on a mission to discover why heathland habitat like this needs to be prized and protected. Margherita goes in search of the sand lizard, one of Britain's rarest reptiles, while Matt is on the hunt for the nightjar. For Adam Henson it is a time of new beginnings as he reopens the farm park to visitors and celebrates the arrival of a litter of rare piglets, while Tom Heap investigates whether brewers and hop growers can recover from the impact of coronavirus.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Salford's MediaCityUK, and meets Blue Peter presenters past and present to discuss the contents of the time capsules buried on the show. Among the items brought in to be valued are a drawing by LS Lowry on the back of a restaurant bill, an 18th-century puzzle jug, and a collection of Second World War badges

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Lata continues to pursue a romantic relationship with Kabir Durrani, but the news reaches her mother, who takes action to keep the young lovers apart. Removed to Calcutta, Lata's spirits are low, until she attends a party hosted by the sophisticated and eccentric Chatterji family. Meanwhile, Saeeda Bai and Maan's affair continues, becoming a source of gossip that threatens them both. As Maan's father rounds on him under growing political pressure, Saeeda Bai takes decisive action of her own

programme.title: Dangerous Liaisons (1988)
programme.desc: A philandering aristocrat in 18th-century France schemes with his manipulative former lover to seduce a virtuous young woman. As their conspiracy becomes ever more tangled, events take an unexpected turn. Stephen Frears' Oscar-winning drama of sexual politics, based on Pierre Choderlos de Laclos' novel, starring John Malkovich, Glenn Close, Michelle Pfeiffer, Swoosie Kurtz, Uma Thurman and Keanu Reeves

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The students are called into the gymnasium for an impromptu assembly, where the Eagle announces some shocking news that hits everyone hard

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The friends turn their attention to solving the mystery, and when the students return from winter break the gang decides to execute a prank to end all pranks

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate a case of starvation and neglect from 1877 that saw a husband stand trial for the murder of his wife

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of properties in Dalton-Le-Dale in County Durham, Irlam in Manchester and Plumstead in London

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A dad and his 12-year-old daughter are lost overnight on a glacier in freezing temperatures, and a man wakes to find his canal boat is sinking when the bank is breached downstream

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction. Natasha also investigates a mystery item that dates back to the mid-19th century

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Pav takes advantage of an awkward situation, and Max must ask a favour

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Stars of the small screen Jeremy Edwards, Chizzy Akudolu, Susie Amy, Mark Little and Hayley Tamaddon take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a newlywed couple in Shropshire, who have £400,000 to buy a country home that will give them room in the future for a growing family. While in the county, Jules visits Weston Park, an estate shaped by the historic pioneer of landscape design, Lancelot 'Capability' Brown

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich brothers reveal their favourite makeovers and also offer gardening tips and ideas. The first edition features a selection of family gardens, including a weird and wonderful Alice in Wonderland-inspired makeover, and one with a basketball court created for two children with mobility issues

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst go off on a search for items, setting out from the South Downs and heading towards an auction in Christchurch on the Dorset coast

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey reports from Bradford Royal Infirmary to find out how the pandemic is transforming the way pregnancy and birth are being dealt with by medical staff. She meets pregnant women terrified of getting the virus, women giving birth and new mums with coronavirus who have to isolate themselves from friends and family. Stacey also speaks to midwives and doctors, who reveal how their working and personal lives have changed in order to protect mothers, babies and themselves from the risk of infection

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Jake Wood and Scott Maslen, aka brothers Max and Jack Branning, who reflect on their time on the Square and look back at the lives and many, many love interests of their characters. Scott tells the story of his cloak and dagger first meeting about joining the show, Stacey shocks them both with a clip of Jake's first-ever appearance in Walford 30 years ago and the pair reminisce about the fateful night of the live 25th anniversary episode of the long-running soap

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison finds herself hounded by the ghosts, who now realise she can both see and hear them, and tries to ignore their existence - a tactic which only exacerbates the problem when the Captain puts the ghosts onto a war footing. Meanwhile, Mike's attempts to turn the house into a home leads to a DIY disaster, saddling Lady Button with an ill-suited roommate, and Julian learns about his questionable legacy when the spooks discover the internet. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: DS Steve Arnott is transferred to a police anti-corruption unit and assigned to investigate senior officer DCI Tony Gates. Not only is his target one of the most popular cops on the force, but his squad has returned the best crime figures for three consecutive years. Surely no one can be that good? Arnott soon finds himself in a cat-and-mouse struggle as he slowly unearths his superior's secrets. The first episode of Jed Mercurio's crime thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston, Vicky McClure, Gina McKee and Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: Kazim is finally moving out of his business partner's flat, but he hasn't worked out where he's going to go yet so heads to his mother's in a bid to make a plan. Meanwhile, Jeremiah works out his relationship with Layla, and some familiar faces turn up

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Guest host Mary Walsh joins judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie on the judging panel as the 'snatch game' arrives to test the remaining contestants

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Broadcaster David Dimbleby takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with investigative journalist Stacey Dooley and comedian Henning Wehn joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the murder of a reclusive pensioner in Scotland in 1952, for which a farm labourer was hanged, despite claiming to have an alibi

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Norwich, Brynmawr in south Wales and Maidstone in Kent

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles examines the extraordinary rescue of a young man missing overnight after being swept out to sea on his surfboard. Plus, a local resident comes to the aid of a young woman who topples into a city-centre river during a night on the town with friends

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, where experts Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt help two teams to bag a bargain or two to take to auction. Plus, Eric uncovers the intriguing story behind a collection of postcards of the First World War era

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia's time off is interrupted alarmingly, and Laila's schooling causes conflict

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Sophie Faldo, David Domoney, Frances Tophill, James Tanner and Will Kirk form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a retired couple search for a new home in rural Somerset with a budget of £700,000, and firm ideas on the vision for their perfect country home. Along the way, Jonnie masters the thrilling sport of land yachting at Brean Sands, where thanks to a prevailing wind he picks up the pace along this picturesque stretch of beach

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: The team recalls some of their favourite quirky garden rescues, including a music festival-inspired plot featuring an air-raid shelter, and a fusion of Yorkshire and Japan

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the New Forest and Southampton Water, and learn about tide milling. Items under the hammer include a mantle clock and a pocket watch

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The 2007 Christmas episode, in which the truth is out about Max and Stacey. It must have seemed like the perfect way to end a lovely Christmas dinner - the Branning and Slater clans settling down together to watch Bradley and Stacey's wedding DVD. Unfortunately, the families weren't expecting to see the blushing bride kissing her new father-in-law as he begged her to resume their illicit affair. Now, everyone has to digest this alarming revelation along with their turkey - and no doubt it's going to be wronged spouses Tanya and Bradley who will be hit the hardest

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric's operation takes place on the same day as Guy's disciplinary hearing, forcing him to choose between treatment that could his life and his need to see Guy face the consequences of his actions. Sacha's silent treatment leaves Essie convinced he knows something about Frankie's disappearance, and Chloe tries her hand at Internet dating - only for one of her matches to be admitted as a patient

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Gates is desperate to sever his ties with Jackie after being duped into covering up her crime, but he is plunged into an even more dangerous situation as Arnott and Fleming close in on his secret and is forced to choose between his marriage and his career. Crime thriller about a detective sergeant working in anti-corruption who is assigned to investigate a respected senior officer. Starring Lennie James, Martin Compston, Vicky McClure, Neil Morrissey and Gina McKee

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Alan Cumming and Nick Grimshaw are among the special guests joining Michelle for a special Pride episode. Chef Jaime Laurita drops in to bake a special pride cake, and Michelle performs a long-distance marriage ceremony for a couple in Scotland

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of reconnecting with nature. She tries giving up environmentally destructive habits like driving and using plastic packaging and going camping in the Welsh Hills, where she meets a family who have embraced the delights of living off-grid

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler demonstrates how to grow herbs and flowers and use them as ingredients in a selection of dishes, including herb omelettes, bread with rosemary and salads. She also reveals how they are important to attract wildlife to gardens, something essential to the health and productivity of any allotment

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's food archives continues as he reaches the letter U. He highlights Heston Blumenthal's recipe for sail porridge and Nigel Slater's method for cooking a black pudding sausage roll

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani visit Chichester Harbour, the only Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in the UK that is managed by a harbour authority, discovering how the needs of a very busy harbour are balanced with those of nature and wildlife. While Matt is out on the water doing the daily rounds with the harbour master, Anita finds out about projects helping to protect local populations of oysters, terns and seals. Plus, Adam Henson's Highland bull Archie might not have long left the farm, but his legacy lives on

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don refreshes a container of overgrown herbs, plants alpines in a pot and revisits the shrub and perennial cuttings he took a few weeks ago. Arit Anderson travels to Kent to meet a designer who is placing sustainability and the environment at the heart of the gardens she designs. Plus, a garden in Dorset that has been laid out specifically with scent in mind and an archive report by Frances Tophill who last year visited an allotment on the Isle of Man to meet a grower who uses plants to make cosmetics and there's a second chance to meet an enthusiast on the Isle of Wight who has filled his garden with daylilies

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter reveals the container potatoes she planted in the first episode, George Anderson harvests his cabbage and blackcurrants and Chris Beardshaw checks on the progress of his box blight battle and explains the art of cloud pruning

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Day three gets under way at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first-round matches continue, featuring Neil Robertson v Liang Wenbo and Yan Bingtao v Elliott Slessor

programme.title: The Crucible: 40 Golden Snooker Years
programme.desc: Steve Davis goes back to the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield to celebrate 40 years of the world snooker championship, talking to some of the players forever ingrained in the sport's history. The venue has become the home of snooker since the championships moved there in 1977, helping to create memorable moments and scenes of drama that live on to this day. Featuring interviews with former champions Stephen Hendry and Dennis Taylor, six-time runner-up and fans' favourite Jimmy White, and celebrity snooker addicts Richard Osman and Stephen Fry

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the first-round matches, as Ronnie O'Sullivan faces Thepchaiya Un-Nooh, and John Higgins takes on Matthew Stevens. O'Sullivan and Higgins have nine world titles between them and Higgins has reached the final here for the last three years, only to end as runner-up on each occasion, while the Rocket is aiming to make a much better fist of his challenge this time, having endured a dismal run of results in the championship in recent years

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session in Sheffield features the concluding session of two first-round matches - Neil Robertson v Liang Wenbo and David Gilbert v Kurt Maflin. Robertson started last year's championship as one of the favourites following an outstanding season, but the Thunder from Down Under came a cropper against eventual runner-up John Higgins, while Gilbert achieved a career highlight by reaching the semi-final, where he also came unstuck against the Wizard of Wishaw

programme.title: The Edge (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about England's Test cricket team between 2009 and 2013, the first and only English side to ever reach the top of the world rankings. Featuring contributions from Andrew Strauss, Kevin Pietersen, Alastair Cook, James Anderson, Stuart Broad, Monty Panesar, Graeme Swann, Jonathan Trott, Matt Prior, Ian Bell, Paul Collingwood, Steven Finn, Tim Bresnan, David Saker and Andy Flower

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Fantasy drama series about people with extraordinary abilities, including a tormented artist (Santiago Cabrera) who paints images of the future, a high-school cheerleader (Hayden Panettiere) and a young dreamer (Milo Ventimiglia) who believes he can fly. In the first episode, an Indian professor travels to New York to solve the mystery of his father's death

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Policeman Matt Parkman discovers he is the only person at the scene of a crime who can hear the muffled cries of a girl, Claire receives unwanted attention and Peter comes close to learning the truth about his brother Nathan. Elsewhere, Hiro finds a comic book starring himself and goes in search of the author, while Niki attempts to piece together what happened during her blackout. Fantasy drama, starring Greg Grunberg, Hayden Panettiere and Ali Larter

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the evening session on day three in Sheffield, featuring Neil Robertson v Liang Wenbo and David Gilbert v Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the first-round matches on day three, where former champions Neil Robertson, John Higgins and Ronnie O'Sullivan were among the players in action

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: A look at the impact of the insurgency from the perspectives of American soldier Lt Col Nate Sassaman and Baghdadi Alaa Adel, who was 12 when she suffered life-changing injuries. Unprepared for the hostile environment he found himself in, Sassaman was pushed to the very darkest regions of his psyche. Alaa was hit in the face by shrapnel from one of the first roadside bombs planted by insurgents and intended for American forces. Looking back at that time, both question the benefits of the war in Iraq

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron is concerned that charismatic locum Drew Nicholson-Heath (guest star John Barrowman) is out to expose him, so he resorts to his old tricks of playing the hero - but he is almost caught out and gaslights a colleague into thinking she made a mistake. A desperate Sacha wants to give Essie a reason to keep on living, but when she insists on preparing for the inevitable, he does something completely out of character. Kian spends the day babysitting Emma, hoping to win back Jac's heart

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein QC and Sasha Wass QC re-investigate the case against farm labourer William Dumbleton, who was charged with the murder of travelling watch repairman John Edmonds in 1880. The accused man didn't have any legal representation until the commencement of his trial, which lasted just one day. Dumbleton was found guilty and sentenced to death

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The family is caught in a storm and forced to take shelter in a rival group's den - which causes a ruckus when the inhabitants return. Meanwhile, group leader Flower evicts one of the young females in an aggressive fashion

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo tours the imperial city of Hue, crosses the former demilitarised zone that once separated North from South Vietnam, and takes a boat ride on the Red River Delta. Approaching Vietnamese capital Hanoi aboard the Unification Railway, Michael passes the famous street where the locomotive passes within centimetres of the houses either side of the tracks. He also visits a former Soviet factory that once supplied military uniforms to the North Vietnamese army and Viet Cong, which now makes sportswear for the United States Olympic team. He ends this leg at the limestone islets and turquoise waters of Halong Bay

programme.title: Westminster in Review
programme.desc: David Cornock presents a review of how Parliament has tackled the coronavirus crisis

programme.title: Lost Land of the Jaguar
programme.desc: Steve Backshall reaches the summit of a table-top mountain, after climbing a vertical cliff face nearly as high as the Empire State Building. Meanwhile, Dr George McGavin and Gordon Buchanan have to haul three boats and all their kit through the jungle after finding the rapids of the Rewa River impassable. At the end of the journey, George presents a report on the expedition to the President of Guyana, Bharrat Jagdeo

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Ronnie O'Sullivan and Jack Lisowski are the seeds in action at the start of day four in Sheffield, with Thepchaiya Un-Nooh and Anthony McGill their respective opponents

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Attention switches to the afternoon session at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where former champion Shaun Murphy begins his campaign against Noppon Saengkham, and Yan Bingtao concludes his first-round match against Elliott Slessor. Bingtao is seeded here for the first time in his career, but has only qualified for the championship on one occasion, and has yet to win a match on the sport's biggest stage

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: One of the meerkats is poisoned, Mozart is allowed to return to the group, and love is in the air as some of the residents search for mates. Documentary following a family of meerkats living on the edge of the Kalahari desert, narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Volunteers at one of the RNLI's busiest stations on the River Thames, central London are called in the middle of the night to try and rescue two students, who have jumped naked into the river while high on the drug LSD. On the coast in Newquay, a 12-year-old boy on a cub scout 'coasteering' trip has fallen badly and has suspected spinal damage, while volunteers at Eastbourne lifeboat station recover the body of someone who has taken their own life

programme.title: Our Friend Victoria
programme.desc: Maxine Peake explores Victoria Wood's take on appearance, how the comedian took swipes at society's views on how people look, and what they should wear and eat. Comedy clips sum up Victoria's views on the diet and health industry. Plus, Julie Walters and Celia Imrie ponder Acorn Antiques' transformation into a health spa, and Duncan Preston reveals the pain he endured when faced with a wrinkly gusset

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the third of the student quiz's first-round matches, four students from Imperial College London pit their wits against a team from the University of Strathclyde in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: When Iraq's president Saddam Hussein was captured by coalition forces in December 2003, politicians in the White House believed this would be the turning point they needed to bring democracy to Iraq. This edition looks at how Saddam's removal led to a new chapter of conflict as sectarian differences flared up to plunge Iraq into a civil war. Senior CIA analyst John Nixon was the first person to question Saddam, and the dictator's answers during the five-week interrogation left the American both surprised and horrified

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of guests Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Larry Dean, Ed Gamble and Kerry Godliman. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Comedy short, written by and starring Diane Morgan (Philomena Cunk). A woman becomes obsessed with a sofa she has seen in a shop window. Unable to think of anything else, she goes to desperate lengths to possess the furniture of her dreams

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the evening session on day four in Sheffield, where Jack Lisowski faced Anthony McGill and Mark Selby took on Jordan Browne

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the first-round matches on day four, where former champions Ronnie O'Sullivan, Mark Selby and Shaun Murphy were among those in action

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani visit Chichester Harbour, the only Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in the UK that is managed by a harbour authority, discovering how the needs of a very busy harbour are balanced with those of nature and wildlife. While Matt is out on the water doing the daily rounds with the harbour master, Anita finds out about projects helping to protect local populations of oysters, terns and seals. Plus, Adam Henson's Highland bull Archie might not have long left the farm, but his legacy lives on

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: On the Wirral, today's artists create artworks to complement a recently completed house renovation. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The last group of five well-known faces, tennis coach Judy Murray, broadcaster and internet personality Riyadh Khalaf, Olympic gold medallist and sports presenter Sir Matthew Pinsent CBE, TV personality Pete Wicks, and actor and comedian Crissy Rock, enter the MasterChef kitchen to take on a series of culinary challenges. Crissy, Sir Matthew and Pete head to Sam's Riverside, while Judy and Riyadh go to Scully to cook for paying customers in a busy lunch service, before the contestants head back to the MasterChef kitchen to impress judges John and Gregg with their own creations

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich brothers reveal their favourite makeovers and also offer gardening tips and ideas. The first edition features a selection of family gardens, including a weird and wonderful Alice in Wonderland-inspired makeover, and one with a basketball court created for two children with mobility issues

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate a case of starvation and neglect from 1877 that saw a husband stand trial for the murder of his wife

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: One of the meerkats is poisoned, Mozart is allowed to return to the group, and love is in the air as some of the residents search for mates. Documentary following a family of meerkats living on the edge of the Kalahari desert, narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reveals how to pot tomatoes on to the next stage of their growth. He also pots up tender vegetables and plants containers for summer colour. Adam Frost enjoys the seasonal plants in his garden and shares plans to transform a part of his plot into a seating area using a few logs and plants. Arit Anderson fills a range of imaginative containers with a variety of plants, while Frances Tophill explores spring flowering trees and their benefits for bees. Plus, how one man has designed his home around his love of houseplants

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the first fixtures in four months, including St Helens v Catalans Dragons and Huddersfield Giants v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day five in Sheffield, where Shaun Murphy concludes his first-round match against Noppon Saengkham, and Mark Allen v Jamie Clarke begins

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day five at the Crucible Theatre, where Mark Selby concludes his first-round match against Jordan Browne, and Stephen Maguire v Martin Gould continues. Selby is a three-time champion of this event, with his last triumph coming in 2017 when he defeated John Higgins 18-15 in the final, but he was eliminated by Gary Wilson in the second round last year

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: A growing population puts pressure on the Whiskers group, and further stress is created when Big Cy from the rival Lazuli turns up. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Cornwall, the station's newest recruit, 18-year-old Shaunna, is following in the family tradition and her grandfather's footsteps. After a successful first rescue of a teenage holiday-maker, she hopes to become Newquay's first helmswoman. Meanwhile, Oban on the west coast of Scotland also has a new 18-year-old recruit, Andrew. However, he and the rest of the crew are given a painful reminder of the dangers all lifeboat volunteers face when they are woken in the middle of the night to rescue a fishing boat that is sinking

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Cuba's isolation has helped preserve many of its natural riches, creating unspoiled landscapes home to many enchanting animals. In the first of a two-part series, cameraman, director and naturalist Colin Stafford-Johnson explores corners of the country that few outsiders have seen. Amongst the wonders he encounters is the bee hummingbird, the world's tiniest bird, and the spectacle of thousands of crabs migrating en masse. In this authored odyssey, the natural magic of one Earth's most intriguing countries is revealed

programme.title: Cuba: Castro vs the World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary about Fidel Castro's leadership of Cuba, beginning with a look at his rise to power in 1959, which earned the hostility of the US and the support of the Soviet Union, a situation that culminated in the Cuban missile crisis. The programme also examines how Castro's government supported liberation struggles in other nations, including Algeria, Bolivia and Angola

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Danny Bhoy hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, warming up the audience before introducing routines by actor-comedian Miles Jupp and happy-go-lucky chancer Lee Nelson, aka Simon Brodkin

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Ireland. Action from the third and final match of the series, which took place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day five from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where Mark Allen's first-round match against Jamie Clarke reached a conclusion

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the first-round matches on day five, where Shaun Murphy, Mark Allen and Mark Selby were among those in action

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades investigates the UK's espionage history through three items restored on the programme, including a bike and teapot linked to undercover operatives

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Leicestershire to pitch ideas to celebrate the buyers' dog. But only two will go on to realise their artworks, before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining four celebrities face two more challenges, beginning with the pairs task. Here they are split into teams and have to each serve up either John Torode's sushi selection or his veal schnitzel with spaetzle - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at Leyton Orient Football Club

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason to look back on a Prom that made history in 2017 when Chineke!, became the first British majority BME symphony orchestra to take the Proms stage. Including music by Dvorak, Handel and Rimsky-Korsakov, and featuring Kanneh-Mason himself, soprano Jeanine De Bique and conductor Kevin John Edusei

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but experiences engine problems on the way

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor

programme.title: Chas and Dave: Last Orders
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into Chas Hodges and Dave Peacock's successful five decades in the music industry, which has seen them share the same bill as Led Zeppelin and make a big impact on London's cultural landscape. The film blends archive performances with more recent tour footage and features contributions by Pete Doherty, Jools Holland and Phill Jupitus. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: What We Were Watching
programme.desc: Grace Dent explores the sights, sounds and schedules of the Great British Summer, an epic journey that covers everything that informs viewers attitudes to summertime. She looks back at a range of influential television, from the travel shows of the sixties and seventies that first brought the world's finest resorts into our living rooms, to Del and Rodney Trotter fooling about abroad

programme.title: Ibiza: The Silent Movie (2019)
programme.desc: Director Julien Temple and soundtrack provider Fatboy Slim offer a musical time-travelling trip through the history of dance music's most influential, charismatic and spiritual island in the Mediterranean, taking in 3,000 years of its history. Ibiza's bohemian heart is now facing its strongest challenge yet, to continue to beat strongly in the face of the ever growing annual invasion of wealthy socialites and the gentrification of the island in the name of progress

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roxy's date is overshadowed by another mysterious fainting spell, Dawn believes she's found the perfect match for Shabnam and Ian makes amends with Christian

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roxy confesses her shocking secret to Christian, the Masood household is sent into chaos with a visit from Uncle Inzamam, and Shirley riles Pat into whacking her with her crutches

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roxy struggles to make the right decision, but can't bring herself to confide in Ronnie, and a shocking discovery makes Shabnam realise she and Tamwar must find work

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ronnie reveals the demons in her past to a stunned Peggy. Shirley and Heather use their time behind bars to face some home truths, and Shabnam and Zainab open up to each other

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session on day four focuses on Mark Selby v Jordan Browne and Jack Lisowski v Anthony McGill. Three-time champion Selby lost in the second round here last year to Gary Wilson, while Lisowski suffered a first-round defeat to former runner-up Ali Carter

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: In central Thailand's forests, fertile plains and even its city streets, nature finds a way of living alongside people, and spirituality can be found in human and animal relationships, both likely and unlikely. This bustling region is known as the nation's rice bowl - but even here, there are magical places to be found. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Thailand, from the northern city of Chiang Mai to Kanchanaburi on the River Kwai and from Bangkok to the southern beach resort of Hua Hin. Along the way, he tries his hand at the art of umbrella-making and learns about northern Thai cookery. He also stops off to help at a hospital for elephants wounded by landmines and admires the ruined temples at the former Siamese capital, Ayutthaya

programme.title: Games Britannia
programme.desc: Historian and broadcaster Benjamin Woolley explores the increasing popularity of video games, from the early creations of the 1980s including Elite, the boom in the 1990s that led to the invention of characters like Lara Croft, to the present-day online communities created in Runescape and World of Warcraft, in which people can adopt alternative personas

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Three men visit Folsom Prison in California, where they join convicts serving long sentences for murder, assault and robbery for a four-day group therapy session

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke examines the three 'golden ages' of British sculpture and the technical breakthroughs that made them possible. He begins with a look at the Norman ecclesiastical building programme and the masterworks created by anonymous medieval masons and artisans to the glory of God - until Henry VIII's Reformation brought wholesale destruction

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: In central Thailand's forests, fertile plains and even its city streets, nature finds a way of living alongside people, and spirituality can be found in human and animal relationships, both likely and unlikely. This bustling region is known as the nation's rice bowl - but even here, there are magical places to be found. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day five from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where Mark Allen's first-round match against Jamie Clarke reaches a conclusion and Barry Hawkins v Alexander Ursenbacher begins. Allen suffered a shock 10-7 defeat at the hands of China's Zhou Yuelong at this stage last year, but the Northern Irishman has been in consistent form this season and currently holds a career-high world ranking of fourth

programme.title: Empire of the Tsars: Romanov Russia with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines the period between 1825 and 1918, in which four successive tsars faced a Russian society moving rapidly toward revolution. Lucy discovers how the 'Tsar-Liberator' Alexander II tried to free millions of serfs from slavery and examines the rise of radical writers and thinkers who sought to have a voice. She also uncovers the family life of the last tsar, Nicholas II, including the influence of Rasputin and questions whether the killing of the ruler and his family could have been avoided

programme.title: The Treasures of Ancient Rome
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke charts the decline and fall of the Roman Empire through some of its hidden and most magical artistic treasures. On a trip to Leptis Magna in Libya, shortly after the overthrow of Colonel Gaddafi, he finds one of the best preserved Roman cities in the world and the cradle of later Roman art. Sooke discovers glorious mosaics that have never been filmed before, but also finds evidence of shocking neglect of Libya's Roman heritage under the Gaddafi regime

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: A tidal wave has carried Brizan's lighthouse into the forest and an arm of the sea has split the small town in two, a spectacle Lena foresaw in her dreams. The inhabitants panic, but Faust Ketchak sees it as a punishment against the devastating behaviour of people, while Lena and Ben uncover pollution on Brizan's construction site. In French

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The town's mayor calls a meeting to discuss plans to deal with the cloud, which might have a devastating effect on the surfers. In French

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant concludes his exploration of the French Riviera's artistic legacy by focusing on how art and celebrity became intertwined in the early part of the 20th century. Resident artists such as Picasso and Matisse found the sun-drenched landscape ideal for experimenting with their work, and as the region became a creative hub, the likes of novelist Jean Cocteau and ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. This artistic community also liked to party on the Cote d'Azur's beaches - a pastime that proved equally irresistible to the American multimillionaires arriving in the area on board transatlantic liners

programme.title: The Treasures of Ancient Rome
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke charts the decline and fall of the Roman Empire through some of its hidden and most magical artistic treasures. On a trip to Leptis Magna in Libya, shortly after the overthrow of Colonel Gaddafi, he finds one of the best preserved Roman cities in the world and the cradle of later Roman art. Sooke discovers glorious mosaics that have never been filmed before, but also finds evidence of shocking neglect of Libya's Roman heritage under the Gaddafi regime

programme.desc: Justin and his friends go to the hairdressers and Mr Tumble pretends to be a hairdresser

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: All of the Teletubbies are stuck. Tinky Winky's hand is stuck in the bag, Dipsy's hat is stuck, Laa-Laa's ball is stuck and Po is stuck on the Tubby Slidey

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: One Twin Po wants their bedroom to be bright, but the other likes it dark

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Crunch starts a library in her treehouse, but her books are all about nuts. The Olobobs make Book-a-Beep-Beep, who makes books from twigs - any book you can imagine

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: When Pablo meets Mum's friend Fiona, all he notices about her is one bright shiny button. He tells the Book Animals 'it's a Fiona', but they have all seen something different

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Some things are easy to agree on, like snow is cold and the sun is hot. But Hugo finds it harder to agree on other things, like when he wants to play basketball but his friends want to play dolls! They learn to compromise, and go on a fun bumper car ride all together!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Three young brothers have fun blowing bubbles in the garden

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies rally driver Catie shows off three amazing machines that are used on farms

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain knits some new uniforms for the crew

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the Squirrels go on a mission to contain an outbreak.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter, Lily and Benjamin accidentally spill Mittens' milk, they launch a daring mission to replace it

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a bowling alley to find out how it works, and we learn how ribbon is made

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: There is an amazing seahorse merry-go-round on the beach, and everyone is having lots of fun going round and round - except Bevel. When he runs away from the ride, Bob finds him, only for Bevel to admit he's nervous about going in circles. Everyone joins in to help him overcome his fear and soon he's sitting on his seahorse, Blue Yah, excited about his first turn on a merry-go-round. But now the key that winds it up has broken, so Bitz needs to work out how to get it started using wind power

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetties race to save the White Cliffs of Dover

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: When Tim realises his eyesight is not as good as it should be, Mummy arranges for him to have an eye test. Meanwhile, Topsy makes a new friend in hospital and Grandma's dog Mossy has to go to the vet

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle learns that reading can be just as much fun as noisy maze games

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran go to the museum to find a giant fossilised Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The duo make a colourful new friend during breakfast

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Mr Onion rides out on the Bumparoller to help all of those around him

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small Clanger finds a strange ball while out fishing in space. No one knows what it is so Major decides to put it on a shelf in his workshop. But a little while later he finds it in two hollow pieces on the ground - and the Soup Dragon discovers that something has been eating the copper trees. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy lets Igglepiggle hold her special stone while she rides the Ninky Nonk, but he forgets all about it

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Maureen Lipman reads Never Tickle a Tiger, Pamela Butchart's humorous story about a little girl who just cannot keep still

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the final at Wembley Stadium, where Chelsea and Arsenal clashed, looking to provide their respective managers with their first silverware in charge of the clubs. The Blues enjoyed the stronger league campaign, but the Gunners held their rivals to a draw when the sides last met at Stamford Bridge in January

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Ciara McEleney wants to move her whole family to Australia for the better weather. Husband Ruairi, however, is proving to be a cloud on her new horizons. A week in Perth sees them check out the forecast for moving Down Under on a permanent basis. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Halesowen in Birmingham and an end terrace in Northwood in Stoke on Trent are sold under the hammer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall help teams look for items at the Mona Showground in Anglesey, while presenter Anita Manning finds out about Portmeirion pottery

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen sets out on a quest to discover the meaning of art. It's a long strange trip into a world of giant inflatables, edible sculptures and flying doughnuts, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits London's Olympic Stadium to explore the links between Christianity and sport, and JB Gill meets Watford goalkeeper Heurelho Gomes, who is training to be a pastor

programme.title: Scrum V Classics
programme.desc: Ireland v Wales. A look back at the 2008 Six Nations match between the sides at Croke Park, as Wales aimed to clinch the Triple Crown and maintain their hopes of winning the Grand Slam

programme.title: War Horse (2011)
programme.desc: A farmer's son forms a close bond with a horse he trains, but is forced to give the animal up when it is sold to the British army during the First World War. The horse passes through various owners on both sides as he travels across the battlefields of France, while the boy refuses to give up on being reunited. Steven Spielberg's drama based on Michael Morpurgo's book, starring Jeremy Irvine, Tom Hiddleston and Benedict Cumberbatch

programme.title: The Story of Wales
programme.desc: Huw Edwards explores the ancient kings who shaped Wales as a nation, and how their battles with Vikings, Saxons and Normans made the local culture flourish. He then learns how the death of Wales's last native prince Dafydd ap Gruffydd was followed by a century of plague and famine, and what Owain Glyndwr's rebellion against the English Crown meant

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are at Ockham and Wisley Commons in Surrey on a mission to discover why heathland habitat like this needs to be prized and protected. Margherita goes in search of the sand lizard, one of Britain's rarest reptiles, while Matt is on the hunt for the nightjar. For Adam Henson it is a time of new beginnings as he reopens the farm park to visitors and celebrates the arrival of a litter of rare piglets, while Tom Heap investigates whether brewers and hop growers can recover from the impact of coronavirus.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Salford's MediaCityUK, and meets Blue Peter presenters past and present to discuss the contents of the time capsules buried on the show. Among the items brought in to be valued are a drawing by LS Lowry on the back of a restaurant bill, an 18th-century puzzle jug, and a collection of Second World War badges

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Lata continues to pursue a romantic relationship with Kabir Durrani, but the news reaches her mother, who takes action to keep the young lovers apart. Removed to Calcutta, Lata's spirits are low, until she attends a party hosted by the sophisticated and eccentric Chatterji family. Meanwhile, Saeeda Bai and Maan's affair continues, becoming a source of gossip that threatens them both. As Maan's father rounds on him under growing political pressure, Saeeda Bai takes decisive action of her own

programme.title: Dangerous Liaisons (1988)
programme.desc: A philandering aristocrat in 18th-century France schemes with his manipulative former lover to seduce a virtuous young woman. As their conspiracy becomes ever more tangled, events take an unexpected turn. Stephen Frears' Oscar-winning drama of sexual politics, based on Pierre Choderlos de Laclos' novel, starring John Malkovich, Glenn Close, Michelle Pfeiffer, Swoosie Kurtz, Uma Thurman and Keanu Reeves

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The students are called into the gymnasium for an impromptu assembly, where the Eagle announces some shocking news that hits everyone hard

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The friends turn their attention to solving the mystery, and when the students return from winter break the gang decides to execute a prank to end all pranks

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of properties in Dalton-Le-Dale in County Durham, Irlam in Manchester and Plumstead in London

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A dad and his 12-year-old daughter are lost overnight on a glacier in freezing temperatures, and a man wakes to find his canal boat is sinking when the bank is breached downstream

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction. Natasha also investigates a mystery item that dates back to the mid-19th century

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales
