programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Stars of the small screen Jeremy Edwards, Chizzy Akudolu, Susie Amy, Mark Little and Hayley Tamaddon take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a newlywed couple in Shropshire, who have £400,000 to buy a country home that will give them room in the future for a growing family. While in the county, Jules visits Weston Park, an estate shaped by the historic pioneer of landscape design, Lancelot 'Capability' Brown

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich brothers reveal their favourite makeovers and also offer gardening tips and ideas. The first edition features a selection of family gardens, including a weird and wonderful Alice in Wonderland-inspired makeover, and one with a basketball court created for two children with mobility issues

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst go off on a search for items, setting out from the South Downs and heading towards an auction in Christchurch on the Dorset coast

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey reports from Bradford Royal Infirmary to find out how the pandemic is transforming the way pregnancy and birth are being dealt with by medical staff. She meets pregnant women terrified of getting the virus, women giving birth and new mums with coronavirus who have to isolate themselves from friends and family. Stacey also speaks to midwives and doctors, who reveal how their working and personal lives have changed in order to protect mothers, babies and themselves from the risk of infection

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Jake Wood and Scott Maslen, aka brothers Max and Jack Branning, who reflect on their time on the Square and look back at the lives and many, many love interests of their characters. Scott tells the story of his cloak and dagger first meeting about joining the show, Stacey shocks them both with a clip of Jake's first-ever appearance in Walford 30 years ago and the pair reminisce about the fateful night of the live 25th anniversary episode of the long-running soap

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: As Wales begins to emerge from lockdown, this BBC Wales Investigates programme looks at why people from ethnic minority backgrounds have been disproportionately affected by coronavirus. Wyre Davies meets frontline workers and communities left counting the cost, including a family who lost two brothers to Covid-19 in one day, and asks whether more could have been done to protect them

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: DS Steve Arnott is transferred to a police anti-corruption unit and assigned to investigate senior officer DCI Tony Gates. Not only is his target one of the most popular cops on the force, but his squad has returned the best crime figures for three consecutive years. Surely no one can be that good? Arnott soon finds himself in a cat-and-mouse struggle as he slowly unearths his superior's secrets. The first episode of Jed Mercurio's crime thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston, Vicky McClure, Gina McKee and Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: Kazim is finally moving out of his business partner's flat, but he hasn't worked out where he's going to go yet so heads to his mother's in a bid to make a plan. Meanwhile, Jeremiah works out his relationship with Layla, and some familiar faces turn up

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Guest host Mary Walsh joins judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie on the judging panel as the 'snatch game' arrives to test the remaining contestants

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Ellie is a fashion fanatic looking for a guy who shares her passion for clothes and shoes - but will she see past the clothes with Mr Grim, Mr Snaps or Mr Waves

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Katie likes an older man who can keep her on her toes - and magicians, motorbikes and a didgeridoo are just some of the things that she has to look forward to

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the murder of a reclusive pensioner in Scotland in 1952, for which a farm labourer was hanged, despite claiming to have an alibi

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Norwich, Brynmawr in south Wales and Maidstone in Kent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, where experts Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt help two teams to bag a bargain or two to take to auction. Plus, Eric uncovers the intriguing story behind a collection of postcards of the First World War era

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia's time off is interrupted alarmingly, and Laila's schooling causes conflict

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Sophie Faldo, David Domoney, Frances Tophill, James Tanner and Will Kirk form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a retired couple search for a new home in rural Somerset with a budget of £700,000, and firm ideas on the vision for their perfect country home. Along the way, Jonnie masters the thrilling sport of land yachting at Brean Sands, where thanks to a prevailing wind he picks up the pace along this picturesque stretch of beach

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: The team recalls some of their favourite quirky garden rescues, including a music festival-inspired plot featuring an air-raid shelter, and a fusion of Yorkshire and Japan

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the New Forest and Southampton Water, and learn about tide milling. Items under the hammer include a mantle clock and a pocket watch

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The 2007 Christmas episode, in which the truth is out about Max and Stacey. It must have seemed like the perfect way to end a lovely Christmas dinner - the Branning and Slater clans settling down together to watch Bradley and Stacey's wedding DVD. Unfortunately, the families weren't expecting to see the blushing bride kissing her new father-in-law as he begged her to resume their illicit affair. Now, everyone has to digest this alarming revelation along with their turkey - and no doubt it's going to be wronged spouses Tanya and Bradley who will be hit the hardest

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric's operation takes place on the same day as Guy's disciplinary hearing, forcing him to choose between treatment that could his life and his need to see Guy face the consequences of his actions. Sacha's silent treatment leaves Essie convinced he knows something about Frankie's disappearance, and Chloe tries her hand at Internet dating - only for one of her matches to be admitted as a patient

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Gates is desperate to sever his ties with Jackie after being duped into covering up her crime, but he is plunged into an even more dangerous situation as Arnott and Fleming close in on his secret and is forced to choose between his marriage and his career. Crime thriller about a detective sergeant working in anti-corruption who is assigned to investigate a respected senior officer. Starring Lennie James, Martin Compston, Vicky McClure, Neil Morrissey and Gina McKee

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of reconnecting with nature. She tries giving up environmentally destructive habits like driving and using plastic packaging and going camping in the Welsh Hills, where she meets a family who have embraced the delights of living off-grid

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Alan Cumming and Nick Grimshaw are among the special guests joining Michelle for a special Pride episode. Chef Jaime Laurita drops in to bake a special pride cake, and Michelle performs a long-distance marriage ceremony for a couple in Scotland

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The 2007 Christmas episode, in which the truth is out about Max and Stacey. It must have seemed like the perfect way to end a lovely Christmas dinner - the Branning and Slater clans settling down together to watch Bradley and Stacey's wedding DVD. Unfortunately, the families weren't expecting to see the blushing bride kissing her new father-in-law as he begged her to resume their illicit affair. Now, everyone has to digest this alarming revelation along with their turkey - and no doubt it's going to be wronged spouses Tanya and Bradley who will be hit the hardest

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the final at Wembley Stadium, where Chelsea and Arsenal clashed, looking to provide their respective managers with their first silverware in charge of the clubs. The Blues enjoyed the stronger league campaign, but the Gunners held their rivals to a draw when the sides last met at Stamford Bridge in January

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Ciara McEleney wants to move her whole family to Australia for the better weather. Husband Ruairi, however, is proving to be a cloud on her new horizons. A week in Perth sees them check out the forecast for moving Down Under on a permanent basis. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A meadow in Woodville in Derbyshire, an old mill in Stoke in Staffordshire and a terraced house in Mountain Ash in Wales are all sold under the hammer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall help teams look for items at the Mona Showground in Anglesey, while presenter Anita Manning finds out about Portmeirion pottery

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits London's Olympic Stadium to explore the links between Christianity and sport, and JB Gill meets Watford goalkeeper Heurelho Gomes, who is training to be a pastor

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an expat couple who want to return from Cyprus to buy a house in Lincolnshire, and also visits a college to find out about the next generation of farmers

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: After a career serving all over the world, army veteran Jason, his wife Esther, and their four children have finally put down roots - but the trouble is their Suffolk garden is little more than a concrete car park. The couple have a budget of £3,000 to try to create a space where they can enjoy sitting out as a family. As the military is a big part of this family's life, they want to see the resulting garden incorporate some remembrance poppies. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Stowmarket to see how they can help Jason and Esther to realise their dreams

programme.title: Tide
programme.desc: A look at how the organisers of a beach horse race on a tidal island off Ireland's west coast, a surfer training on the north coast, and the skipper of a ferry between the Scottish mainland and the Isle of Skye all have to take the tide into account in planning their lives. Plus, how light-bellied Brent geese nesting in Strangford Lough must study the waves to catch their food

programme.title: War Horse (2011)
programme.desc: A farmer's son forms a close bond with a horse he trains, but is forced to give the animal up when it is sold to the British army during the First World War. The horse passes through various owners on both sides as he travels across the battlefields of France, while the boy refuses to give up on being reunited. Steven Spielberg's drama based on Michael Morpurgo's book, starring Jeremy Irvine, Tom Hiddleston and Benedict Cumberbatch

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are at Ockham and Wisley Commons in Surrey on a mission to discover why heathland habitat like this needs to be prized and protected. Margherita goes in search of the sand lizard, one of Britain's rarest reptiles, while Matt is on the hunt for the nightjar. For Adam Henson it is a time of new beginnings as he reopens the farm park to visitors and celebrates the arrival of a litter of rare piglets, while Tom Heap investigates whether brewers and hop growers can recover from the impact of coronavirus.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Salford's MediaCityUK, and meets Blue Peter presenters past and present to discuss the contents of the time capsules buried on the show. Among the items brought in to be valued are a drawing by LS Lowry on the back of a restaurant bill, an 18th-century puzzle jug, and a collection of Second World War badges

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Lata continues to pursue a romantic relationship with Kabir Durrani, but the news reaches her mother, who takes action to keep the young lovers apart. Removed to Calcutta, Lata's spirits are low, until she attends a party hosted by the sophisticated and eccentric Chatterji family. Meanwhile, Saeeda Bai and Maan's affair continues, becoming a source of gossip that threatens them both. As Maan's father rounds on him under growing political pressure, Saeeda Bai takes decisive action of her own

programme.title: Dangerous Liaisons (1988)
programme.desc: A philandering aristocrat in 18th-century France schemes with his manipulative former lover to seduce a virtuous young woman. As their conspiracy becomes ever more tangled, events take an unexpected turn. Stephen Frears' Oscar-winning drama of sexual politics, based on Pierre Choderlos de Laclos' novel, starring John Malkovich, Glenn Close, Michelle Pfeiffer, Swoosie Kurtz, Uma Thurman and Keanu Reeves

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The students are called into the gymnasium for an impromptu assembly, where the Eagle announces some shocking news that hits everyone hard

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The friends turn their attention to solving the mystery, and when the students return from winter break the gang decides to execute a prank to end all pranks

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate a case of starvation and neglect from 1877 that saw a husband stand trial for the murder of his wife

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of properties in Dalton-Le-Dale in County Durham, Irlam in Manchester and Plumstead in London

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A dad and his 12-year-old daughter are lost overnight on a glacier in freezing temperatures, and a man wakes to find his canal boat is sinking when the bank is breached downstream

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction. Natasha also investigates a mystery item that dates back to the mid-19th century

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Pav takes advantage of an awkward situation, and Max must ask a favour

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Stars of the small screen Jeremy Edwards, Chizzy Akudolu, Susie Amy, Mark Little and Hayley Tamaddon take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a newlywed couple in Shropshire, who have £400,000 to buy a country home that will give them room in the future for a growing family. While in the county, Jules visits Weston Park, an estate shaped by the historic pioneer of landscape design, Lancelot 'Capability' Brown

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich brothers reveal their favourite makeovers and also offer gardening tips and ideas. The first edition features a selection of family gardens, including a weird and wonderful Alice in Wonderland-inspired makeover, and one with a basketball court created for two children with mobility issues

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst go off on a search for items, setting out from the South Downs and heading towards an auction in Christchurch on the Dorset coast

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: The horticultural designer fashions a garden for the Hanway family in Howth, who have a shed-load of ideas for their patch, but no firm plan. For those wanting to live the good life themselves, Diarmuid gets advice from chef Rachel Allen, who helps him fulfil his dream of keeping chickens in the garden

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Jake Wood and Scott Maslen, aka brothers Max and Jack Branning, who reflect on their time on the Square and look back at the lives and many, many love interests of their characters. Scott tells the story of his cloak and dagger first meeting about joining the show, Stacey shocks them both with a clip of Jake's first-ever appearance in Walford 30 years ago and the pair reminisce about the fateful night of the live 25th anniversary episode of the long-running soap

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey reports from Bradford Royal Infirmary to find out how the pandemic is transforming the way pregnancy and birth are being dealt with by medical staff. She meets pregnant women terrified of getting the virus, women giving birth and new mums with coronavirus who have to isolate themselves from friends and family. Stacey also speaks to midwives and doctors, who reveal how their working and personal lives have changed in order to protect mothers, babies and themselves from the risk of infection

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: DS Steve Arnott is transferred to a police anti-corruption unit and assigned to investigate senior officer DCI Tony Gates. Not only is his target one of the most popular cops on the force, but his squad has returned the best crime figures for three consecutive years. Surely no one can be that good? Arnott soon finds himself in a cat-and-mouse struggle as he slowly unearths his superior's secrets. The first episode of Jed Mercurio's crime thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston, Vicky McClure, Gina McKee and Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Northern Ireland on The One Show
programme.desc: Jennie Browne and Joe Lindsay present more films from the magazine programme that were made in and about Northern Ireland, featuring reports on the fairy trees of Antrim, an unsinkable nurse and Bangor sea bins

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: Kazim is finally moving out of his business partner's flat, but he hasn't worked out where he's going to go yet so heads to his mother's in a bid to make a plan. Meanwhile, Jeremiah works out his relationship with Layla, and some familiar faces turn up

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Guest host Mary Walsh joins judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie on the judging panel as the 'snatch game' arrives to test the remaining contestants

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Broadcaster David Dimbleby takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with investigative journalist Stacey Dooley and comedian Henning Wehn joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Is the lockdown turning back the clock on women's lives? With women appearing to bear the brunt of the economic fallout of the pandemic, and the UN describing the worldwide increase in domestic abuse as a 'shadow pandemic' alongside Covid-19, many are asking if this is the biggest ever leap backward for women. And yet female leaders at the helm of various nations have been commended for managing the crisis better than their male counterparts - does that reflect that where women are elected as leaders there is a greater presence of women in positions of power across the board?

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has presented humanity with an almighty shock. Our evermore interconnected and technologically advanced societies are now in lockdown and we are fearful for our health and economic futures thanks to an invisible virus. Stephen Sackur speaks to the Israeli historian and best-selling author Yuval Noah Harari. What 21st century lesson can we draw from the spread of Covid-19? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Tuesday 28 April.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of a statement by Health Minister Nadine Dorries in response to the publication of the Cumberledge Review into medical device and medicine safety. The review looked into how the health system responded to concerns raised by patients about the Primodos hormone pregnancy test, the drug sodium valproate and surgical mesh, from Thursday 9 July

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Some are predicting that despite the easing of the lockdown, the arts will be devastated by the financial impact of the pandemic. What will be the effect on the performing arts and on our culture after months in which the cinemas, theatres, concert halls and galleries have been shut down - with huge uncertainty about how and when they will reopen? How will this seismic shock change our culture? Will people start to consume culture increasingly in the privacy of their own homes? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Saturday 4 July.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In times of crisis, we learn about who we really are, and so it is that the global coronavirus pandemic is revealing truths about humankind and how we balance self and collective interest. Stephen Sackur speaks to writer and historian Rutger Bregman whose book Humankind: A Hopeful History is making waves around the world. Do we humans massively underestimate our capacity to change things for the better? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Wednesday 27 May.

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Is the lockdown turning back the clock on women's lives? With women appearing to bear the brunt of the economic fallout of the pandemic, and the UN describing the worldwide increase in domestic abuse as a 'shadow pandemic' alongside Covid-19, many are asking if this is the biggest ever leap backward for women. And yet female leaders at the helm of various nations have been commended for managing the crisis better than their male counterparts - does that reflect that where women are elected as leaders there is a greater presence of women in positions of power across the board?

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey reports from Bradford Royal Infirmary to find out how the pandemic is transforming the way pregnancy and birth are being dealt with by medical staff. She meets pregnant women terrified of getting the virus, women giving birth and new mums with coronavirus who have to isolate themselves from friends and family. Stacey also speaks to midwives and doctors, who reveal how their working and personal lives have changed in order to protect mothers, babies and themselves from the risk of infection

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: The schoolchildren write articles for the local newspaper and Kate Morag realises trying to find an interesting story is a difficult task

programme.title: Òrain CBeebies
programme.desc: A selection of songs from Carrie, Linda, Ruairidh and the CBeebies ALBA presenters

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Mario is looking forward to eating the big plate of chips Shane has promised him - bit does not realise the chef is working on a healthy version

programme.title: Luthaidh (Louie)
programme.desc: A friendly eagle helps Louie and Yoko shelter from the wind

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The animals visit the seaside, and learn about the word 'igloo'

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Catriona MacPhee reads Mar a Chuireas tu Leòmhann air Falach written and illustrated by Helen Stephens and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: The Baron becomes King of Great Goldlandia, and Danger Mouse is forced to work as his arch enemy's bodyguard

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: When Heather and Windshear land on Dragon's Edge to join the Dragon Rider crew, they quickly discover that Windshear does not play well with others

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Tennis is the latest sport to feature

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Following the activities of Bert, a two-year-old beaver who lives on a section of river in Wyoming's Teton Mountains. The programme follows the rodent as he moves in and out of the log cabin he has built with his mate

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: The community gathers to celebrate the opening of the Lochboisdale marina development, where Angus and Neil have a shellfish stall. Paul attends an athletics competition in Stornoway and there's a fund-raising Halloween tea in Sollas

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Featuring Phil & Aly, with Quendale Bay at the Trad Awards

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Otis the Great Dane faces serious surgery after suddenly becoming paralysed. With damaged discs pressing on his spinal chord, Scott Rigg has to perform delicate surgery

programme.title: Baby Killer? Sgeulachd Jessie King
programme.desc: The story of Jessie King, who at the age of 27 was on trial at the High Court in Edinburgh after she was accused of killing three babies. Despite not being their mother, King had taken them in for money, with no intention of keeping them. Presenter John Morrison invites the opinion of expert contributors

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: The adventure of friends Magnus Graham and Daibhidh Martin, cycling through some of the most remote, hostile and unforgiving terrain in Asia - stunning landscapes, human kindness and a challenging but ultimately amazing experience

programme.title: Ceol Aig A Chaisteal
programme.desc: Traditional tunes from some of Scotland's leading young musicians

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming, heading to the coastal region of Sanna, at the far western tip of the peninsula of Ardnamurchan, in Lochaber

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Hymns, psalms and personal faith stories. Presented by Iain MacKinnon

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Dino Dana
programme.desc: Dino Dana, a spunky eight-year-old palaeontologist in training eats, sleeps and breathes dinos. Eager to attend Fossil Day at the Royal Ontario Museum, Dana asks her neighbour Mr Hendrickson to take her

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is handing out food to the neighbourhood animals when he stumbles on a gigantic hamster that Fizz has released from some parallel dimension.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo can't find the book that Linda lent him and he's ready to turn the house upside down to find it. The problem is that he's actually in Fizz's loft.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's on a trip to Translovenia and takes shelter in a mansion owned by a vampire. In reality, this vampire is just Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: A bear has escaped from the zoo and Fizz is hell-bent on making it his pet. Magoo mistakes the bear for his niece who's come to spend the day with him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Fizz for his best friend Harry from school, and thinks the two of them are visiting their old school.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is forced into action when the raccoons steal the turtles to help Freddy hold up his newly made throne. The crazy raccoon envisions himself a king and thinks the turtles are the perfect finishing touch to his scheme. Mike sneaks into the raccoons' lair to steal back his friends. With Iris's help, he intends to teach the raccoons a lesson they won't forget.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Poor Mike is bedridden. He's been struck down by a nasty cold so Iris and the turtles are forced take care of his every need, including trying to repel the raccoons from the house. Fighting raccoons isn't their specialty and Iris and the turtles quickly end up locked in the garage. Mike is forced to intervene but he's so sick and weak that he's a pushover. How can he overcome the raccoons this time? Mike's problem could be the very solution he needs to defeat the tenacious raccoons.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A piano teacher meets his match in the most inept pupil ever to tinkle an ivory - none other than Mr Bean, who has got it into his head to complete Beethoven's unfinished symphony

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson stops in at a fringe family reunion

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Ronan Keating, Nick Knowles and Claire King join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare their favourite American dishes, including two varieties of fluffy pancakes, pizza base with a stuffed crust, chocolate-chip cookie ice cream sandwiches, and beans served with loaded potato skins

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: The presenter turns her attention to child-friendly baking, making a festive reindeer cake, a gingerbread house cake, and snowmen cakes

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three factories in Yorkshire. It reveals how Aunt Bessie's produces millions of Yorkshire puddings a year, how Yorkshire Tea turn out teabags by the truckload and how Birds Eye workers face a race against time to get their peas from field to freezer. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher continues his journey around the Wales Coast Path as he travels around the wild Llyn Peninsula. He takes the helm of a 1930s sailing ship to navigate the notorious Menai Strait. This narrow stretch of water, which separates Anglesey from the rest of north Wales, has a reputation for being difficult to navigate - but luckily Sean is joined by ex-pilot boat skipper Emrys Williams. Moving on, Sean also hits the waves with adaptive-surfer Llywelyn Williams, who lost his leg in a car accident, and meets a woman who walked the entire path with her donkey

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the final day of the blind auditions in the junior version of the singing talent contest. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Flamingo, Leopard, Flower and Rottweiler perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A motorbike gets stuck up a tree, a woman receives an electric shock from a fly zapper and a pensioner ends up on TV - after the TV ends up on top of them

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a look at the classic pairings and comedy double acts from the soap that have kept viewers entertained through the decades, including Stan and Hilda Ogden, Fred Elliott and Ashley Peacock, and Steve McDonald and Lloyd Mullaney.

programme.title: The Man with the Golden Gun (1974)
programme.desc: James Bond's investigation into the death of a world-renowned scientist working on solar technology draws him into a battle of wits with a stealthy assassin, who dispatches his victims with a golden bullet and has the British secret agent next in his sights. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore, Christopher Lee, Britt Ekland and Maud Adams

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family faces the unwanted arrival of a domineering aunt, while Larry's girlfriend also pays a visit - giving the unwelcome relative an opportunity to voice her disapproval of the whole family. Sven makes an offer that helps Louisa cope with adversity and Margo falls in love - but Gerry is more concerned with his newly acquired owlets

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Neville Chamberlain visits Downton for dinner, and is drawn into Violet's plan to save the hospital, with distressing consequences for everyone. Andy finds an unexpected confidante while helping Mr Mason move into Yew Tree Farm, Mary is uncertain if she has met her match, Edith meets Bertie Pelham in London, and Spratt comes to Denker's aid. Mrs Hughes struggles to keep Carson happy, while Mary thinks secrets are being kept from her

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The house is made open to the public for a day to raise money for the hospital, an act that divides opinions among the family and the servants. Tensions between Violet and Cora come to a head as a decision is made about the hospital takeover, Mr Dawes has a proposition for Molesley, and Baxter is thrown into turmoil by an unexpected letter. Carson is not happy with Thomas, but all is not as it appears, while Mary and Edith hope to overcome their past and find new happiness with the men in their lives

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Documentary allowing a peek at the workings the Ritz, one of the most famous luxury hotels in the world. Executive chef John Williams prepares to launch the first-ever cookbook that reveals The Ritz's most famous recipes. Penny Lancaster pops in for cocktails and head concierge Michael De Cozar is celebrated for his 46 years of service

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Two years ago, the programme brought together 20 foundlings from across the UK, united in their hope of finding birth relatives. Through DNA and detective work, the show's experts have been helping them find the answers they've been searching for. In this edition, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell catch up with three of them to find out how their lives have changed since they discovered the truth about their pasts and how their new relationships are developing

programme.title: Gone to Pot: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Christopher Biggins and Bobby George are feeling the effects of a little over-indulgence after a few too many medicated mouthfuls at Nonna Marijuana's kitchen. John Fashanu and Linda Robson, both parents themselves, meet a mum who claims medical marijuana has transformed her daughter's life and, later, the group entertain at their Hollywood base, where they meet a chef who uses marijuana in cakes

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the dealers visit Walsall in the West Midlands, where items of interest include old photographs of West Bromwich Albion FC, an unusual walking stick and some Moorcroft pottery.

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five radio presenters from Sussex answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Priya confronts Sarah, Harriet is stopped in her tracks, and Dan lashes out at Amelia

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Police ask Sarah about the accident, and why she called 999 before the collision happened. In Roy's Rolls, Elaine introduces herself to Sally, and explains that she is Tim's mother. And Jenny sympathises with Abi over the twins' imminent departure for Australia

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As Tracey is released from prison, Sharon welcomes her sister home with the offer of a job in the school kitchen, where she's now manager. Meanwhile, Dorien's gambling problem is getting out of hand - she's broke and can't pay the rent - so Sharon again comes to the rescue. Linda Robson, Pauline Quirke and Lesley Joseph star in the comedy

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a look back at the soap's scandalous storylines, with illicit affairs, teenage pregnancy and bigamy having all have rocked the street over the years. Among the stories featured are Sarah Louise Platt's teenage pregnancy and the Baldwin family's tangled affairs

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall ,Nicky Campbell and the team help 67-year-old Christine Rose yo search for the sisters she was separated from at the age of four when her mother was prosecuted for neglect. The three young girls were taken by social services during the night and sent to separate children's homes. Nicky leads the search for Christine's sisters and the team makes remarkable discoveries, revealing layers of family that she never knew existed

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: Previously unseen footage from the actress's travelogue programmes, beginning with a look at her journey from Japan to Siberia. The trip begins on the frozen Japanese sea of Okhotsk, and goes across China and Mongolia before arriving at Lake Baikal in Siberia, where Joanna meets some amazing seals

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a selection of interviews, clips and profiles from the ITV archives featuring some of the biggest names in sport. In the first edition, Muhammad Ali discusses his ban from boxing and the Rumble in the Jungle, and takes part in an impromptu sparring session, while Brian Clough has a heated on-screen debate with Don Revie, his predecessor at Leeds. Plus, a revealing portrait of Welsh rugby talisman Alun Wyn Jones

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen Ashraf demonstrates her recipes for lamb burgers, Chappal kebabs, and marinated tandoori chicken, while in India she meets a food historian, who prepares a modern version of an old royal Mughal dish. Parveen also takes chef Alex Moser and a group of hoteliers to a famous roadside eatery in Delhi and discovers the origins of butter chicken

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson presents the show from Cheltenham racecourse, where items brought in to be valued include a pistol, a cluster ring, and a car boot cabinet

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A family from Swansea attempt to top the Tenable tower and win a big cash prize by answering top 10 list questions in the daily quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Joe, Phoebe, Ross and Rachael pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: With Louisa visiting her mother in Spain to get some perspective on their marriage, Martin knows he needs to try to change for the sake of their relationship, so he endeavours to keep his promise to her to see a therapist. Aunt Ruth arranges an appointment for him with Dr Rachel Timoney, but a lifeboat training exercise causes problems for the doctor. Will he get back in time for his first session? Martin Clunes stars

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson welcomes more contestants to the studio, all keen to answer 15 questions correctly in the hope of winning £1,000,000 - with lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, Ask the Host and 50:50 available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: The actress introduces more unseen footage from her travelogue programmes, this time focusing on her journeys to India and Uzbekistan. Joanna shares some previously unseen moments and provides viewers with an insight into the making of the series. In this episode, she travels the length of India, including footage of her encounters with the flower-sellers of Madurai, before taking us back to Uzbekistan, where she reveals some gory tales of the country's history and discovers the secrets of gem-making

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Anne: The Princess Royal at 70
programme.desc: A special documentary marking Princess Anne's 70th birthday, as she talks openly about her life and work, and the reality of growing up as a member of the Royal family. Throughout the programme, Princess Anne talks frankly about a family in which women tended to be seen as 'honorary men', about the challenges for younger royalty, about a bloody kidnap attempt, and about refusing titles for her children, Peter and Zara

programme.desc: Text-based information service

In this edition, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell catch up with three of them to find out how their lives have changed since they discovered the truth about their pasts and how their new relationships are developing

programme.title: Gone to Pot: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Christopher Biggins and Bobby George are feeling the effects of a little over-indulgence after a few too many medicated mouthfuls at Nonna Marijuana's kitchen. John Fashanu and Linda Robson, both parents themselves, meet a mum who claims medical marijuana has transformed her daughter's life and, later, the group entertain at their Hollywood base, where they meet a chef who uses marijuana in cakes

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As Tracey is released from prison, Sharon welcomes her sister home with the offer of a job in the school kitchen, where she's now manager. Meanwhile, Dorien's gambling problem is getting out of hand - she's broke and can't pay the rent - so Sharon again comes to the rescue. Linda Robson, Pauline Quirke and Lesley Joseph star in the comedy

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a look back at the soap's scandalous storylines, with illicit affairs, teenage pregnancy and bigamy having all have rocked the street over the years. Among the stories featured are Sarah Louise Platt's teenage pregnancy and the Baldwin family's tangled affairs

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall ,Nicky Campbell and the team help 67-year-old Christine Rose yo search for the sisters she was separated from at the age of four when her mother was prosecuted for neglect. The three young girls were taken by social services during the night and sent to separate children's homes. Nicky leads the search for Christine's sisters and the team makes remarkable discoveries, revealing layers of family that she never knew existed

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: Previously unseen footage from the actress's travelogue programmes, beginning with a look at her journey from Japan to Siberia. The trip begins on the frozen Japanese sea of Okhotsk, and goes across China and Mongolia before arriving at Lake Baikal in Siberia, where Joanna meets some amazing seals

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a selection of interviews, clips and profiles from the ITV archives featuring some of the biggest names in sport. In the first edition, Muhammad Ali discusses his ban from boxing and the Rumble in the Jungle, and takes part in an impromptu sparring session, while Brian Clough has a heated on-screen debate with Don Revie, his predecessor at Leeds. Plus, a revealing portrait of Welsh rugby talisman Alun Wyn Jones

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen Ashraf demonstrates her recipes for lamb burgers, Chappal kebabs, and marinated tandoori chicken, while in India she meets a food historian, who prepares a modern version of an old royal Mughal dish. Parveen also takes chef Alex Moser and a group of hoteliers to a famous roadside eatery in Delhi and discovers the origins of butter chicken

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures

programme.title: Anne: The Princess Royal at 70
programme.desc: A special documentary marking Princess Anne's 70th birthday, as she talks openly about her life and work, and the reality of growing up as a member of the Royal family. Throughout the programme, Princess Anne talks frankly about a family in which women tended to be seen as 'honorary men', about the challenges for younger royalty, about a bloody kidnap attempt, and about refusing titles for her children, Peter and Zara

programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present two stories of women trying to bring their families back together. Mother and daughter Val and Marisa Moorhouse from West Yorkshire are united in their search for their missing son and brother Stephen, whom Val had adopted in 1977, when she felt she could not afford to give him the life he deserved. Meanwhile, 37-year-old Sam from Surrey is searching not only for her birth mother, but also her sister

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: Joanna begins a 2,000-mile journey across Japan in Hokkaido, where she meets one of the most important animals in Japanese culture, the red-crowned crane. She arrives in Sapporo during the middle of the annual Snow Festival and meets members of the local indigenous community, before travelling into the Fukushima exclusion zone and taking a bullet train from Nagano to Tokyo

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Charlotte and Sidney clash again, only for Charlotte to be left confused by a chance encounter. Tensions surrounding Miss Lambe's arrival in Sanditon erupt at Lady Denham's grand pineapple luncheon, as the hostess makes disparaging comments about Miss Lambe's race, and Esther and Edward realise that Clara poses a significant threat to their ambitions. Drama adapted from the unfinished novel by Jane Austen, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Anne Reid, Charlotte Spencer, Kris Marshall and Kate Ashfield

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: In Hampshire, Becky spends her days as governess to Sir Pitt Crawley's neglected daughters, but her ambition is to get into her employer's good books and soon she is promoted to the position of his secretary. When Sir Pitt's sister Matilda pays a visit to Queen's Crawley, Becky forms a new plan and quickly charms her way to becoming her favourite new companion. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel, starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Two children go on trial for killing a toddler, each blaming the other, and the case hinges on whether forensic evidence can prove who is guilty of strangling the boy to death. CPS director George Castle defies his superiors and takes instruction from the victim's mother, who has a surprising point of view on the crime. Drama, guest starring Deborah Findlay (Cranford), with Bill Paterson

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old girl is found dead in her home, Brooks and Devlin cannot be sure a crime was even committed. On further investigation, an unlikely murder weapon is uncovered and a suspect identified - a recently released serial rapist. Determined the accused is guilty, Steel pursues him with every power available - pushing the boundaries of the law to the limit. Crime drama, guest starring Kevin Doyle (Drop Dead Gorgeous), with Ben Daniels

programme.title: Davina McCall: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: Davina travels to the Norwegian archipelago of Svalbard in the Arctic Circle, a harsh environment set across 15 million sq km of sea ice where temperatures can drop to -40C. She learns how huskies' thick fur is better insulation than goose down, that the deep pads on their paws protect them from the cold and their almond shaped eyes mean they are less exposed in a blizzard. She also tracks a polar bear to the open sea and witnesses bearded seals using their whiskers like feelers to find shrimp and crab on the ocean floor

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Drama unfolds in the garage, Cain and Moira grow closer, and Charity is frustrated by another dead end

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah enacts a plan on Nicola's advice, Andrea is in a state of bliss, and Jamie's mask slips

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily sees Ashley leaving Ken's first thing in the morning. Deirdre discovers Alec is short staffed and offers her services. Steve tells Fiona he was wrong to go off with Vicky

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve and Vicky go before the Magistrates charged with perverting the course of justice. Ken tells Kelly she must be careful about what goes on in his house

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is a tense day in the Glover household when the inquest into Dave's death begins. Chris urges Frank to prove that James is his child by getting a paternity test

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Seth forgets Betty's birthday leading him to pretend he has a surprise planned. Jan apologises for hitting Kathy. Zak tidies the Dingle household after inviting Lisa for dinner

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Steve Crane takes an urgent phone call, and is shocked to discover Peter Pyke's warehouse has gone up in smoke. It soon emerges that the owner's wife had spent time behind bars for fraud, and her husband is reluctant to answer questions - leading Merton to suspect the blaze was deliberate. Meanwhile, Vernon moves into his new mansion, but has trouble keeping up with his well-to-do neighbours. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby challenges Philip to a boxing match, convinced Miss Jones will throw herself at his feet if he wins. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby discovers there is a new man in Miss Jones's life and decides to declare his feelings for her - but his romantic gesture comes too late. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert, Angie and Ida's move north goes from bad to worse when they eventually arrive only to discover the furniture hasn't

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz Merrick faces a trying time when one of her patients misses an appointment to discuss medication, and subsequently ends up charged with drink-driving, while Vernon's pal David is kidnapped and held to ransom. Merton is delighted Jenny has agreed to marry him, and Bellamy finds out that Gina is carrying his baby. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Geoffrey Hughes and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic goes on the hunt for Kelly when she does not come home, Frank offers Kim her money to get out of his life, and Zak asks Marlon and Butch to help Lisa on the pig farm

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is devastated to find out that Frank is James's father. Viv reassures Donna that Kelly will be okay. Marlon hatches a plan to sell Lisa's pigs to a butcher

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack worries about the horse's colic as he is off his food. Vicky feels Steve is too clever for her, fearing he will blame her for everything and walk free

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Duckworths book a holiday in Lloret del Mar, but struggle to find someone to look after the pub. Alec threatens Steve with the police for breaking the conditions of the court

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz Merrick faces a trying time when one of her patients misses an appointment to discuss medication, and subsequently ends up charged with drink-driving, while Vernon's pal David is kidnapped and held to ransom. Merton is delighted Jenny has agreed to marry him, and Bellamy finds out that Gina is carrying his baby. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Geoffrey Hughes and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As Morse sees in 1970 at a Venetian opera, Thursday and the team are faced with a grisly murder on an Oxford towpath. Back in Oxford, Morse meets a kindred spirit, while a ground-breaking college project becomes a fatal battle of the sexes. Shaun Evans stars as the cerebral detective, in an episode he also directed, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser and Sean Rigby

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Three people are murdered in a shop in central London and a fourth is left for dead, and Brooks and Devlin are up against the clock to find a killer who they fear could strike again. When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: In a retrospective episode set immediately after the events of series two, Rachel faces further accusations of masterminding the fatal attack on ex-lover Nick and finds comfort in the arms of ever-faithful Sean, agreeing to marry him. Amid the wedding celebrations, Janet and Ade admit defeat and decide to split up for good, while Gill is duty bound to take action when she catches Pete in a compromising position. The detectives also investigate the case of a businessman suspected of murdering a homeless man

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet

programme.desc: Text-based information service

Merton is delighted Jenny has agreed to marry him, and Bellamy finds out that Gina is carrying his baby. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Geoffrey Hughes and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: A smooth-talking new tenant called Seymour, whose aristocratic veneer conceals the soul of an out-and-out crook, tries to con Rigsby out of his life savings. Classic comedy, with Leonard Rossiter and Henry McGee

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby invests in some new furniture in a desperate attempt to impress Ruth's replacement, Brenda, but makes the mistake of opting to pay for it on the never-never. Leonard Rossiter and Gay Rose star

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida finds her new home uncomfortable, until a special delivery arrives

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An old friend of Merton is sent from CID to help track down a bank robber, and he agrees to be best man at the wedding, while Jenny's disgraced brother Steve tries to patch things up with his sister before she ties the knot. Events conspire to jeopardise the special day and stop the happy couple making it to the church on time. Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey, Mark Jordon and Tricia Penrose star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly tries to hitch a lift, and Biff tells Kim to leave the village as she is not wanted. She does not want anything more to do with James now Kim knows Frank is the father

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy encourages Donna and Robert to bunk off school. Vic insists that Kelly tells him all about Tom. Butch and Marlon are confronted by a mystery man at Lisa's pig farm

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec urges Vicky to report Steve to the police for harassment, Jim warns Steve that he is on the road to being a loser, and Rita agrees to go to York with Fred

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Billy and Betty look after the Rovers while the Duckworths go on holiday. Maureen is stunned when Reg's girlfriend Yvonne Bannister confronts her

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An old friend of Merton is sent from CID to help track down a bank robber, and he agrees to be best man at the wedding, while Jenny's disgraced brother Steve tries to patch things up with his sister before she ties the knot. Events conspire to jeopardise the special day and stop the happy couple making it to the church on time. Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey, Mark Jordon and Tricia Penrose star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. As DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new forensic pathologist Kam Karimore investigate the mysterious crime, they find themselves drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching during their quest to identify the perpetrator. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Helen Baxendale and Navin Chowdhry

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a wealthy man whose double life left him vulnerable to exploitation. Away from work, Rachel's recent moment of indiscretion threatens to come back to haunt her when a confused Sean takes a phone call from her one-night stand, and after Ade is pushed into living with his girlfriend on a permanent basis, Janet asks her mother to move in

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ed is gutted that Aggie has to isolate at a hotel on their 30th wedding anniversary, and Sarah demands to know if Gary killed Rick, before he is hit by a car. Elaine calls Alya and confirms that she's prepared to give evidence against Geoff

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fights for his life, and Adam senses Sarah is lying about her showdown with her ex. Meanwhile, Alya implores Elaine not to leave as she is the only hope of securing Yasmeen's freedom, and Ed sits down to a virtual anniversary dinner with Aggie via video call

programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. programme.desc: The Duckworths book a holiday in Lloret del Mar, but struggle to find someone to look after the pub. Alec threatens Steve with the police for breaking the conditions of the court

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz Merrick faces a trying time when one of her patients misses an appointment to discuss medication, and subsequently ends up charged with drink-driving, while Vernon's pal David is kidnapped and held to ransom. Merton is delighted Jenny has agreed to marry him, and Bellamy finds out that Gina is carrying his baby. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Geoffrey Hughes and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As Morse sees in 1970 at a Venetian opera, Thursday and the team are faced with a grisly murder on an Oxford towpath. Back in Oxford, Morse meets a kindred spirit, while a ground-breaking college project becomes a fatal battle of the sexes. Shaun Evans stars as the cerebral detective, in an episode he also directed, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser and Sean Rigby

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Three people are murdered in a shop in central London and a fourth is left for dead, and Brooks and Devlin are up against the clock to find a killer who they fear could strike again. When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: In a retrospective episode set immediately after the events of series two, Rachel faces further accusations of masterminding the fatal attack on ex-lover Nick and finds comfort in the arms of ever-faithful Sean, agreeing to marry him. Amid the wedding celebrations, Janet and Ade admit defeat and decide to split up for good, while Gill is duty bound to take action when she catches Pete in a compromising position. The detectives also investigate the case of a businessman suspected of murdering a homeless man

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet
