programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby invests in some new furniture in a desperate attempt to impress Ruth's replacement, Brenda, but makes the mistake of opting to pay for it on the never-never. Leonard Rossiter and Gay Rose star

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida finds her new home uncomfortable, until a special delivery arrives

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An old friend of Merton is sent from CID to help track down a bank robber, and he agrees to be best man at the wedding, while Jenny's disgraced brother Steve tries to patch things up with his sister before she ties the knot. Events conspire to jeopardise the special day and stop the happy couple making it to the church on time. Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey, Mark Jordon and Tricia Penrose star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly tries to hitch a lift, and Biff tells Kim to leave the village as she is not wanted. She does not want anything more to do with James now Kim knows Frank is the father

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy encourages Donna and Robert to bunk off school. Vic insists that Kelly tells him all about Tom. Butch and Marlon are confronted by a mystery man at Lisa's pig farm

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec urges Vicky to report Steve to the police for harassment, Jim warns Steve that he is on the road to being a loser, and Rita agrees to go to York with Fred

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Billy and Betty look after the Rovers while the Duckworths go on holiday. Maureen is stunned when Reg's girlfriend Yvonne Bannister confronts her

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An old friend of Merton is sent from CID to help track down a bank robber, and he agrees to be best man at the wedding, while Jenny's disgraced brother Steve tries to patch things up with his sister before she ties the knot. Events conspire to jeopardise the special day and stop the happy couple making it to the church on time. Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey, Mark Jordon and Tricia Penrose star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. As DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new forensic pathologist Kam Karimore investigate the mysterious crime, they find themselves drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching during their quest to identify the perpetrator. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Helen Baxendale and Navin Chowdhry

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a wealthy man whose double life left him vulnerable to exploitation. Away from work, Rachel's recent moment of indiscretion threatens to come back to haunt her when a confused Sean takes a phone call from her one-night stand, and after Ade is pushed into living with his girlfriend on a permanent basis, Janet asks her mother to move in

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.desc: Coverage of the delayed season-opener, which features three races at Donington Park. programme.title: World of Sport

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a selection of interviews, clips and profiles from the ITV archives featuring some of the biggest names in sport. In the first edition, Muhammad Ali discusses his ban from boxing and the Rumble in the Jungle, and takes part in an impromptu sparring session, while Brian Clough has a heated on-screen debate with Don Revie, his predecessor at Leeds. Plus, a revealing portrait of Welsh rugby talisman Alun Wyn Jones

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Arsenal v Manchester United. With commentary from Brian Moore and Brian Clough

Road comedy, with Burt Reynolds, Jackie Gleason, Jerry Reed, Dom DeLuise and Sally Field

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An antiques shop is used as a point of access in a bank robbery, and it looks like an inside job, but Regan suspects otherwise and soon finds himself on a collision course with Haskins. Police drama, starring John Thaw, Dennis Waterman and Garfield Morgan

programme.title: The Saint

programme.desc: A series of robberies is committed on affluent couples travelling down a country lane, and it transpires two of the suspected offenders are wealthy themselves. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

He plans lamb pasanda and tandoori monkfish, but Tammy Rice finds something in her glass that wasn't on the menu. However, Josh hopes his disco entertainment might impress the guests enough to bag him the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tammy Rice hosts the final evening in Dumfries and Galloway, hoping to win the £1,000 prize with her no-nonsense approach and traditional Scottish fare. The dinner-party novice rustles up chorizo and cous cous stuffed peppers for her starter and incorporates haggis into her chicken-based main course

programme.title: Countdown

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: Marge has plastic surgery in an effort to lose weight, but a mix-up results in her being given breast implants instead - much to Homer's delight. With the guest voices of Adam West and Burt Ward, stars of 1960s TV series Batman

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge signs Homer and Bart up on a father-son bonding ocean voyage in a bid to heal their troubled relationship, and while the pair are away takes over Homer's fantasy football league. Animation, featuring the voice of Nick Offerman (Parks and Recreation)

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Anneliese Dodds MP sets out the Labour Party's view on defending British high street businesses

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken and Dr Helen Lawal challenge three pairs of friends to try out the latest popular diets, while cook Stacie Stewart demonstrates some healthy recipes. Sophie and Zoe give the Citrus Diet and the Sexy Pineapple Diet a road test, Barbara and Denise spend six weeks on the Summer Salad Diet and the Summer Sandwich Diet ahead of their holiday in Ibiza, and Theo and Ervis take on the Greek Island Longevity Diet and the South American Diet so they can lose weight in time for a pool party

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Another selection of memorable stories filmed at King's College and St George's hospitals in south London, with this edition focusing on patients and staff who have all overcome personal setbacks and adversity. Eve, 83, has fallen down the stairs into her basement and has an open ankle fracture that needs urgent realignment to restore the blood flow to her leg, while four-year-old Maddi has fallen over and injured her neck on a train

programme.title: The Talk
programme.desc: Famous parents of black and mixed race children share their personal stories of making them aware of racism, and being given similar lessons by their own parents. Tinie Tempah, Emeli Sandé, Leigh-Anne Pinnock, Lennie James, Maro Itoje and Ade Adepitan are among the contributors discussing being on both sides of this commonplace exchange, and how they dealt with subjects like being seen as different or dealing with the police

programme.title: Race Against the Virus: The Hunt for a Vaccine
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Covid-19 spread unstoppably throughout the world, told through the eyes of key scientists on the pandemic frontline. Personal diaries recorded by scientists and intensive care doctors give an insight into the race to identify the virus and try to unlock the secrets of its virulence, revealing how scientists came to realise the seriousness of the disease. Racing to develop a vaccine, leading scientist Professor Sarah Gilbert discusses the pressures she faces and her hopes that a vaccine can be developed

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the Prohibition Grille in Everett, Washington, where he meets belly dancer-turned-restaurateur Rishi Brown, who is making all the wrong moves with her business. Distracted from the task of improving her menu by performing for customers, she's oblivious to the disastrous dishes being served from her own kitchen

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples host dinner parties in the Spanish resort of Marbella as they competes for the cash prize of £1,000. Stephen and his Colombian boyfriend Juan plan to keep things simple in the kitchen, but two of their guests point out that the chicken is overcooked. At Glenn and Henriette's Mexican evening, the contestants get competitive when they're let loose on a pinata, while Tristan and Louise hope to inject love into their evening - until Stephen gives them frank feedback on the food

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Cornish adaptive surfer Pegleg plans to extend his VW campervan by creating a new cabin from scratch, while Martin and Carol-Anne want to build a glamping pod in their Pembrokeshire garden, using a dome that originally housed an electricity generator at the London Olympics. George also visits an earthquake-resistant glass house on the coast of western Japan, and looks at aluminium panels as a new kind of canopy for his treehouse

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The story of Lesley and Mark Irwin, who fell foul of a single mother and confidence trickster. After several months, the tenant fell behind with the rent and it was discovered she had advertised the home as a rental property and stole deposits from unwitting students hoping to move into the area. Plus, a family living in a cramped and cold multiple-occupancy house in south London, and have to share a bathroom and kitchen with other tenants, seek help from Lewisham Council

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Ben Reeve from Lewisham Council's Rogue Landlord Unit aims to help Lola, who stopped paying rent 18 months earlier in an effort to get her negligent landlord to deal with her dilapidated flat's many problems. Eviction specialists also come to the aid of Amit, who found himself continuing his late father's battle with a nightmare renter, and 70-year-old landlady Joan, who started receiving unnerving threats from her rent-skipping tenant after she started legal proceedings to get him evicted

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The efforts of north London landlord Franc to evict a tenant withholding rent payments on a property whose boiler mysteriously stopped working whenever the rent was due, and a tenant with asthma who has been forced to sleep on his sofa for two years in a damp flat. Plus, a landlord owed £7,000 in rent discovers her nightmare is not over when she evicts the tenant and gains access to her property

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: Return of the documentary series following hill shepherds Amanda and Clive Owen and their nine children. This third series begins as the family bids farewell to Raven as she leaves for university. Four-year-old Clemmy is also about to start school - but is less keen on leaving the farm, especially since it means less time with her beloved pony Tony. The family all has to pull together when Clive goes into hospital for hip replacement surgery, preventing him from working on the farm as winter draws nearer

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: Amanda and the children prepare for the demands of breeding season in autumn. However, with Clive recovering from hip surgery and Raven away at university, Amanda and the rest of the children face a particularly demanding set of challenges. The season provides a valuable sex education lesson for Violet and Edith. Reuben studies for his tractor-driving test, Clive buys a new bull, and Clemmy takes a ride on a miniature Shetland

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: All nine children return home for Christmas. Clemmy has her hands full nursing a premature calf, while Clive has fixed up an ancient contraption to cook the festive roast. When an owl is sighted in one of the barns, the family comes up with a plan to encourage more birds to roost there. Miles rigs up a night vision system to keep an eye on their guest, while Sidney traps pests to lure it out

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Hansen and Gittings families swap homes and budgets for the week - and although they both live in Bournemouth, their financial circumstances differ greatly. Yoko and Richard Hansen live in a five-bedroom house with their one child, take up to four overseas holidays a year and enjoy regular trips to see West End musicals. Their weekly budget is more than £1,300. Sarah and Greg Gittings, meanwhile, live in a two-bedroom flat, and get by on just £116 a week

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: The programme follows Graham, a pest controller who battles critters, bugs and vermin across the Costa del Sol, as he goes on a snake hunt and is called to an ant infestation. Plus, a pair of Welsh ex-pats who moved to Spain to run a bar, and the so-called 'cat lady of the Costa del Sol', who feeds more than 100 street cats as well as taking them to the vets to be neutered

programme.title: Britain's Flashiest Families
programme.desc: Documentary about the habits and lifestyles of people who are unashamedly excessive spenders. In Doncaster, South Yorkshire, multimillionaires Noelie and Robin Goforth are not afraid to splash their cash, from liposuction to glamorous dinner parties and a £375,000 boat, while TV presenter Lizzie Cundy spends her days buying glamorous dresses and attending red carpet events alongside A-list stars. Leanne Couch admits to forking out up to £10,000 a year spoiling her prized 12-year-old Maltese terrier, and the cameras follow her as she prepares for an event to celebrate the opening of her high-end dog-themed bakery

programme.title: Husbands from Hell
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on partners who give their wives grief, including Alan in Oxfordshire who turned the family garden into a scrapyard and just cannot stop buying cars - 120 of them at the last count. Jane from Dunfermline has ended up with a Mr Bean lookalike for a husband thanks to her partner Bill, while opera singer Nicky in Bury has to put up with her husband's obsession with cleaning the house at the crack of dawn

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth: How the Other Half Lives
programme.desc: Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford shed more light on the lives of the super-rich, taking their investigation beyond British shores to meet some of the wealthiest people on the planet. They begin with a trip to New York, which is reportedly home to more billionaires than anywhere else in the world. Eamonn introduces Mr Chatwal and his son Vikram, who run a hotel empire together, and are rich enough to enjoy treats such as 1,000-dollar ice cream sundaes covered in edible 24-carat gold. Ruth heads to the Hamptons, one of the most exclusive stretches of real-estate in the world, and meets party planner Andrea Correale, who caters to the weird and wonderful requests of a string of A-list clients. Finally, the take in New York's Upper East Side, where they interview the charismatic Mrs Barsky, who has lived in an apartment in the luxurious Carlyle Hotel for the past 23 years

programme.title: Tribal Teens - Here Comes Trouble
programme.desc: British teenagers get the wake-up call of a lifetime as they are challenged to live among the world's toughest tribes, tasked to survive in some of the planet's most extreme and hostile environments. Unmotivated 18-year-old Ethan and 16-year-old pampered princess Alex are flown deep into the Peruvian rainforest, home of the Ashaninka tribe. There are no let-ups, no lie-ins and no luxury as the pair are forced to forage for food in the oppressively humid conditions with only the most basic of living conditions provided

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines how trains transformed the nation and shaped modern Britain. He begins in the cab of Puffing Billy, the world's oldest-surviving steam locomotive, which was built in 1813 and designed to move coal along a five-mile stretch of track from a Northumberland mine to the docks. Chris also tells the story of Henry Booth, who not only championed George Stephenson's famous Rocket, but also helped finance it, and talks about the work of the navvies who dug the tunnels and laid the tracks

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice offers advice to would-be sellers in Cotham, Bristol, whose four-bedroom bungalow has been on the market for four months. She suggests revamping the shabby sitting room to enhance its ambience and updating the mismatched kitchen with a lick of paint, tile jewellery and new flooring, before turning her attention to sprucing up the two bathrooms

programme.desc: George and Peppa take part in a treasure hunt organised by Granny and Grandpa Pig

programme.title: Mya Go
programme.desc: When a thunderstorm keeps Sammy awake, Mya Go tells him a story to help him sleep. Cartoon for pre-school children

programme.title: Fireman Sam
programme.desc: Pontypandy Fire Station is opened to the public and Norman is eager to try things out, crawling under safety tape and getting into the driving seat of a fire engine. However, his inquisitive nature and desire for excitement lead to danger when he climbs onto the roof to get closer to Tom and his helicopter

programme.title: Shane the Chef

programme.title: Abby Hatcher
programme.desc: Mrs. Melvin's hairbrush goes missing, and Abby tracks it to a brand new Fuzzly who loves to style hair

programme.title: Daisy & Ollie
programme.desc: The friends pretend to be space explorers and wonder what aliens might really look like

programme.title: Milkshake! Monkey's Amazing Adventures
programme.desc: The character uses his imagination to find out how the Romans lived and, with the help of archaeologist Rob as the Milkshakers make their own Roman buildings

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The piglet and her family visit a new theme park, where there is a lot of fun to be had with vegetables

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Daddy Pig takes Peppa and George to visit a house that Mr Bull is building

programme.title: Ricky Zoom
programme.desc: When Ricky is busy playing with Dasher and leaving his friends out, the other buddies form a new club with Toot

programme.title: Pirata & Capitano
programme.desc: Master map-maker Hippo-the-Wise needs Pirata's help to find Sun Treasure Island. But as the Pink Skull draws near, mermaids try to lure them away

programme.title: Paw Patrol
programme.desc: Daring Danny X, dressed as a bird mascot, gets carried away by a couple of eagles who mistake him for one of their own

programme.title: Paw Patrol
programme.desc: The residents of Adventure Bay are working hard to win the record for the world's tallest tower of pizzas

programme.title: The Secret Life of Puppies
programme.desc: Four Cockerpoo puppy sisters head off to the vets for the first time for their puppy injections and learn to play and wrestle with each other

programme.title: Wissper
programme.desc: Dan the panda usually likes to be alone, but he asks Wissper to help him find a friend

programme.title: Floogals
programme.desc: Mum and Dad Hooman are in the garden laying out a colourful egg hunt for the children, but the Floogals think they have dropped the eggs by accident and tidy them up

programme.title: Sunny Bunnies
programme.desc: The gang plays on a slide, but finds it takes too long to get back up to the top

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
programme.desc: The broadcaster and guests discuss the issues of the day, with co-host Storm Huntley joining him for phone-ins and reading out viewers' correspondence

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, agents Matt and Garry chase £1,700 owed by an accountant to a client, and officers Max and Paul try to recover almost £30,000 in north west London owed in rent arrears by a dentist after he was barred from working. Meanwhile, agents Max and Steve head to south west London to chase almost £2,000 owed by a cafe owner

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Susan and Gary bought an East End flat to let out and were approached by a letting agent who promised hassle-free guaranteed rent. But before they knew it, he had broken in and changed the locks. Now they are fighting to get the property back. Meanwhile, landlord Paul discovers that his seriously ill father's long-standing tenant owes a staggering £20,000 in rent for living in her Notting Hill home

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: When Jasmine learns of the kidnapping, she is quick to admonish Tori, and Colby tries to give Nikau the benefit of the doubt, but Tane remains hostile

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: David and Aaron are thrown by the arrival of Emmett's brother, Brent. Karl's concern for Jane increases, so he does some snooping before making a mysterious phone call

programme.title: The Neighbourhood Nightmare (2018)
programme.desc: When a mother falls for the handsome new bachelor on the block, she begins to fear she and her daughter's lives may be in grave danger. Thriller, starring Julie McNiven, Trevor Donovan and Helena Mattsson

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler and Ross face two bullies at Central Perk. Monica decides to have a flutter on the stock market and Phoebe meets a long-lost family member. Comedy, guest starring Giovanni Ribisi (Avatar)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The pals plan a surprise party for Rachel, but her recently separated mother and father show up. To keep the parents apart, the gang decides to have two different gatherings. Comedy, guest starring Marlo Thomas and Ron Leibman

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: David and Aaron are thrown by the arrival of Emmett's brother, Brent. Karl's concern for Jane increases, so he does some snooping before making a mysterious phone call

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: When Jasmine learns of the kidnapping, she is quick to admonish Tori, and Colby tries to give Nikau the benefit of the doubt, but Tane remains hostile

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall treats a rescue dog that steals food and barks at the front door - but finds the training techniques for one problem do not work quite as well with the other. Elsewhere, a Jack Russell with strong reactions to traffic makes going for a walk a potentially lethal experience, and two akitas have an aggressive dislike of all other dogs, which the owners fear may turn violent

programme.title: This Week on the Farm
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Jules Hudson present the final episode in the show in which farmers document their day-to-day lives. A vet comes to Cannon Hall farm to determine if Jeremy the bull has impregnated any cows. Rob and Dave try their hands at making a traditional Yorkshire tipple and take a trip to Whitby to catch some fish. Plus, The Yorkshire Vet's Julian Norton demonstrates traditional sheep shearing methods and farmers in Kent race against time to harvest a crop of rapeseed before the weather changes

programme.title: The Deceived
programme.desc: Michael instructs Mary to keep an eye on Ophelia, but she slips away to meet up with Sean - who guesses that Michael is the father of her baby, but promises to keep her secret. An encounter with a local psychic leads to a surprising announcement. Michael travels to Cambridge when he receives word that his father has taken ill, and goes in search of a mysterious visitor

programme.title: The Best of Bad TV
programme.desc: The unbelievable excesses of 1980s television, including the blissfully ignorant sexism of Miss World, baffling game shows for dogs, the inappropriateness of Minipops and the shape-shifting of Manimal. First-hand contributors, perpetrators and victims, as well as the simply gobsmacked, look back as they recall the joy and the horror of it all

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth: How the Other Half Lives
programme.desc: Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford meet Dr Mohammad Zahoor and his wife Kamaliya in Kiev, Ukraine, to see how the extravagant billionaire couple have spent some of their fortune, including on 12 houses across the globe, a fleet of colour-coded Bentleys, armed security and attack dogs and an expansive wardrobe filled with designer shoes, dresses and handbags. Ruth also meets designer Jody Bell at the luxurious Wellesley Hotel in London, to discuss her quest to become a high-street name in the fashion world

programme.title: Tribal Teens - Here Comes Trouble
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old nightclub dancer Courtney and 17-year-old ultra-vain Charlie face up to life on the frozen Mongolian plains as they spend a week with a family of hardy Kazakhs in a communal tent. The pair consume the local cuisine - including horse meat - and contend with only the most basic sanitation. They also learn to appreciate that for the Kazakh tribe, even getting water involves a major expedition to a frozen river to hack out blocks of ice

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines the role of the railways during the two global conflicts of the 20th century, keeping vital supply lines open and transporting troops to and from the front. In the First World War, railway works were converted into munitions factories and women were employed for these dangerous jobs and their heroic efforts helped win them the vote, while in the Second World War, they were vital in organising the evacuation of a million children and offered underground shelters from air raids

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice suggests improvements for a four-bedroom dormer bungalow in Clapham, West Sussex, to make it more appealing to prospective buyers. The lurid wall colours and pink living room carpet are banished, leaving pine floorboards that help create a sense of the outdoors

programme.title: The Thirty-Nine Steps (1978)
programme.desc: An innocent man is informed of a devious plan to start the First World War by murdering a foreign minister. Almost immediately, he is framed for murder and embarks on a perilous cross-country trek to prove his innocence, pursued by both the police and ruthless spies. Thriller - the third film adaptation of John Buchan's novel - starring Robert Powell, Karen Dotrice, John Mills and George Baker

programme.title: Spy Interview Special
programme.desc: Melissa McCarthy, Miranda Hart and Jude Law, and Bridesmaids director Paul Feig talk about their new action-comedy Spy, and the art of making funny movies

programme.title: Night at the Museum: Battle of the Smithsonian (2009)
programme.desc: The living exhibits at New York's Museum of Natural History are shipped to Washington's Smithsonian Institution - along with the artefact that brought them to life in the first place. Nightwatchman Larry tries to retrieve it, only to find the contents of the world's largest museum have been reanimated, including a pharaoh determined to take over the world. Fantasy comedy sequel, starring Ben Stiller, Amy Adams, Owen Wilson and Steve Coogan

programme.title: The Core (2003)
programme.desc: A chain of bizarre and deadly incidents in several countries leads scientists to the conclusion that the Earth's core has stopped spinning. As chaos erupts around the world, experts hatch a plan to halt the destruction and save the human race - but it involves venturing deep below the planet's surface to detonate a nuclear bomb. Sci-fi thriller, starring Aaron Eckhart, Hilary Swank, Stanley Tucci and DJ Qualls

programme.title: Spy Interview Special
programme.desc: Melissa McCarthy, Miranda Hart and Jude Law, and Bridesmaids director Paul Feig talk about their new action-comedy Spy, and the art of making funny movies

programme.title: Howards End (1992)
programme.desc: A chance encounter during a cruise throws the romantic Schlegel sisters into the paths of the Wilcoxes, an industrious family headed by the well-meaning but strait-laced Henry. When aristocratic matriarch Ruth Wilcox bequeaths her family home to Margaret Schlegel, her children are outraged and dispose of the will, setting the stage for a conflict with tragic consequences. Merchant Ivory period drama, based on EM Forster's novel, starring Emma Thompson, Anthony Hopkins and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Red 2 (2013)
programme.desc: Former CIA agent Frank Moses reunites his squad of retired operatives for a vital mission. A devastatingly powerful new weapon has been stolen, and it falls to Frank's team to retrieve it - which means facing lethal assassins, terrorists and corrupt government officials out to steal the device. Action thriller sequel, starring Bruce Willis, Helen Mirren and John Malkovich

programme.title: Serena (2014)
programme.desc: A couple move to North Carolina during the Great Depression to set up a logging empire. While their business proves successful, various revelations create tension between them and ruthless ambition drives both to violent and destructive ends. Period drama based on the novel by Ron Rash, starring Bradley Cooper, Jennifer Lawrence and Rhys Ifans

programme.title: Love, Rosie (2014)
programme.desc: Rosie and Alex have been best friends since childhood, but face being separated when they head to different universities. As Rosie fears for the strain distance will place on their relationship, she begins to wonder if she wants something more than friendship from him. Romantic comedy based on Cecelia Ahern's novel Where Rainbows End, starring Lily Collins and Sam Claflin

programme.title: Punk Chef on the Road
programme.desc: Punk Chef Scott Garthwaite travels to Northern Ireland to enjoy comfort food. He tastes an Irish stew, local cake and tries his hand at Basketball with a deaf team

programme.title: Fantastic Voyage (1966)
programme.desc: A medical team is miniaturised and injected into the bloodstream of a scientist to repair his severely damaged brain - unaware that one of the microscopic passengers has sabotage on his mind. Richard Fleischer's sci-fi adventure, starring Raquel Welch, Stephen Boyd, Donald Pleasence and Edmond O'Brien

programme.title: Animal Farm (1954)
programme.desc: Animals rebel against their unscrupulous owner and take control of his farm, vowing to run things fairly, with equality for all. However, the pigs leading the revolt succumb to greed and corruption, culminating in violence and oppression. Animated drama based on George Orwell's novel, narrated by Gordon Heath and with Maurice Denham providing the voices for all the characters

programme.title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty (2013)
programme.desc: An ordinary man seeks solace from his mundane life by drifting into daydreams where he is a heroic figure. When he and a friend are faced with unemployment, he is propelled into a globetrotting adventure in real life. Comedy drama based on James Thurber's short story, directed by and starring Ben Stiller. With Kristen Wiig, Adam Scott and Shirley MacLaine

programme.title: Akeelah and the Bee (2006)
programme.desc: A clever girl from a poor neighbourhood finds school frustrating and tries to hide her intelligence from her classmates. When she competes in a spelling competition, she is surprised to win and with the aid of a strict coach, sets out to take part in a national tournament and provide her community with something to rally round. Drama, starring Keke Palmer, Laurence Fishburne and Angela Bassett

programme.title: Keeping Up with the Joneses (2016)
programme.desc: A suburban couple become embroiled in an international espionage plot when they discover that their seemingly perfect new neighbours are actually government spies. Action comedy, starring Zach Galifianakis, Isla Fisher, Jon Hamm and Gal Gadot

programme.title: Spy (2015)
programme.desc: A deskbound CIA agent provides valuable background support for a dashing secret agent, but has never gone into action herself. Everything changes, when the spy mysteriously vanishes, and she leaps into action to take his place, infiltrating the inner circle of a ruthless arms dealer and trying to avert a global catastrophe. Comedy adventure, starring Melissa McCarthy, Jason Statham and Rose Byrne

programme.title: Mr & Mrs Smith (2005)
programme.desc: An outwardly bored suburban couple lead double lives as professional assassins, both unaware of their partner's secret. When they are hired to kill each other, the deadly situation puts some much-needed excitement back into their stale relationship. Comedy adventure, starring Brad Pitt, Angelina Jolie, Vince Vaughn and Adam Brody

programme.title: Hitchcock (2012)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the making of Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho. The film-maker is forced to fund the controversial project personally in the face of disinterest from the studios, while the financial strain and his lecherous behaviour begin to take their toll on his relationship with wife and creative partner Alma Reville. Starring Anthony Hopkins, Helen Mirren and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Run Wild, Run Free (1969)
programme.desc: Withdrawn, mute 10-year-old Philip is ignored by his parents and goes off to explore Dartmoor alone, where he meets a retired colonel who shares his knowledge of Devon's wildlife. Under the older man's nurturing tutelage, the boy begins to tame a wild white colt, but fate proffers a series of setbacks just as real progress is being made. Children's drama, starring John Mills, Mark Lester, Sylvia Syms and Bernard Miles

programme.title: Rugrats in Paris: The Movie (2000)
programme.desc: The lucky little tots embark on a trip to a French theme park - but while the rest of the gang are intent on enjoying themselves, Chuckie is distracted by his wish for a new mum. He sets his sights on the owner of the amusement park as a prospective partner for his single dad. Animated adventure, with the voices of Christine Cavanaugh, Elizabeth Daily, Cheryl Chase, Kath Soucie and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda 2 (2011)
programme.desc: Martial arts master panda Po continues to hone his skills when an evil peacock, who was banished from China for trying to build a deadly new weapon, returns to cause more chaos. It falls to Po to defeat him, but he suffers strange flashbacks revealing a connection between his past and the sinister bird. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jack Black and Gary Oldman

programme.title: The Pink Panther Strikes Again (1976)
programme.desc: Inspector Clouseau fends off a murder syndicate led by his former police chief Dreyfus, who has been driven insane by his hatred for the bungling detective. However, doing away with the lucky lawman proves harder than anticipated - even for the world's best assassins. Comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Herbert Lom, Lesley-Anne Down, Burt Kwouk and Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Only the Brave (2017)
programme.desc: Eric Marsh, the superintendent of a group of elite firefighters, is pushed to his limit when he and his crew tackle a historic wildfire. Fact-based drama, starring Josh Brolin, Jennifer Connelly and Andie MacDowell. Directed by Joseph Kosinski, helmer of Tron: Legacy and Top Gun: Maverick

programme.title: Roman J. Israel, Esq. (2018)
programme.desc: A driven, idealistic defence attorney gets mixed up in a series of events that lead to a crisis and the necessity for extreme action. Crime drama, starring Denzel Washington, Colin Farrell and Carmen Ejogo

programme.title: 2 Guns (2013)
programme.desc: Two undercover agents - one working for the DEA the other for the Navy - infiltrate a drug cartel, only for the planned sting operation to go disastrously wrong. Both operatives become fugitives and must work together to survive - but both keep their true identity a secret, mistakenly thinking the other is a real criminal. Action adventure, starring Denzel Washington and Mark Wahlberg

programme.title: For Those in Peril (2013)
programme.desc: The sinking of a fishing boat from a Scottish coastal village leaves only one survivor. The superstitious locals blame him for the deaths of the other men and ostracise him, but he refuses to believe that the rest of the crew, including his own brother, are dead and tries to find a way to save them. Drama, starring George MacKay, Kate Dickie and Michael Smiley

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of a French restaurant in the heart of Moorpark, California. The pompous, over-complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away and there is an imminent threat of bankruptcy. However, the owner is vigorously opposed to change, despite the failings of his current business model

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of Lela's, a California restaurant that is currently being run into the ground by its inexperienced owner. Gordon tries to turn the failing business around, but faces opposition from the chef and makes enemies when he exposes the suspect behaviour of some members of staff

programme.title: Supernanny US
programme.desc: Ben Corry is away for months at a time with the army, leaving stay-at-home mum Maria to discipline their four rowdy daughters. Jo Frost brings this military family into line

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Chris decides to turn his wedding into the ultimate obstacle course, but his fiancee Charlotte dreams of a wedding day filled with family tradition

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Former boxing champ and groom-to-be Jordan plans a fight-night wedding in front of thousands of people, but his fiancee Danielle dreams of a girly and intimate wedding day

programme.title: Kubo and the Two Strings (2016)
programme.desc: A boy unwittingly summons an evil spirit to wreak havoc on his peaceful village home. The only hope of defeating the vengeful creature is to find a magical suit of armour belonging to the boy's father - but finding it will entail matching wits with an assortment of monstrous foes. Animated adventure, with the voices of Charlize Theron, Art Parkinson and Matthew McConaughey

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Comedy clips showcasing amusing internet videos, featuring a pensioner and a parked car, a bear playing with a bamboo stick, and a Street Fighter impression

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As the academic year comes to a close, Barry and Erica wonder how they will appear in the school yearbook and pressure Adam, who is on the team producing it, to see the rough layout before it goes to print. Murray is annoyed when Marvin pays a visit to ask for money. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica cannot wait to get out of the house as Graduation Day approaches - until it dawns on her how much her mother's affection means to her. Barry's plans for a summer with Lainey turn to dust, while Dana makes an unexpected visit. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Hayley Orrantia

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly and Murray help Erica move into her college dormitory, while Adam tries to find the perfect girlfriend for Barry

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam has to tackle a school project on the Second World War, so decides to make a film featuring his grandfather. However, Pops takes matters into his own hands when Adam finds all his war stories mundane. Erica fails to cope with her own cooking and laundry at college and returns home to confront Beverly for over-mothering her, but Murray insists that Beverly starts to teach Erica and Barry basic life skills

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys become stranded in the middle of nowhere when Leonard's car is stolen en route to the Bakersfield comic-book convention. Meanwhile, back at home, the women try to work out why the guys are so fascinated with such publications

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is furious when the university forces him to work with Kripke, while Howard's hobby lands him in financial trouble. However, that doesn't stop him and Raj getting expensive action figures of themselves made - much to Bernadette's disapproval

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: An argument between Leonard and Sheldon over the ending of a Harry Potter book affects Amy and Penny's living arrangements. Meanwhile, Howard goes on a trip to Las Vegas with Bernadette and asks Raj to keep an eye on his mother while he is away

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny celebrate Valentine's Day, but their evening takes a turn for the worse when the latter's ex-boyfriend proposes to his girlfriend in the same restaurant. Meanwhile, Sheldon enlists the help of his assistant Alex to find the perfect gift for Amy

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj is upset after Lucy runs away in the middle of their date, leaving the gang to cheer him up. However, when Howard bumps into her, she admits she is incredibly shy. Meanwhile, Sheldon asks Penny to help with a new episode of Fun with Flags, and because he is impressed by her acting ability, the friends go to see her in a performance of A Streetcar Named Desire

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon cannot contain his jealousy when he hears that his university colleague Bert has won a prestigious fellowship with an impressive grant. Elsewhere, Howard finds an old remote-controlled toy he made in the past, but the rest of the group do not seemed to be too impressed by it. Stephen Hawking guest stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon, Amy, Leonard and Penny do their best to divide up all their possessions into two separate households, but tension ensues. Raj and Stuart compete to become indispensable to Howard and Bernadette as the baby's arrival looms. Christopher Lloyd (Back to the Future) guest stars

programme.title: The Adventures of Tintin (2011)
programme.desc: A reporter's purchase of a model ship brings him under threat from a ruthless criminal, as it is one of a series of clues to the location of the real vessel, which holds a pirate's lost treasure. He sets off on a globetrotting quest to find it, assisted by a drunken sea captain, two bumbling detectives and his faithful dog. Steven Spielberg's animated adventure based on Herge's comic-strip series, with the voices of Jamie Bell, Andy Serkis and Daniel Craig

programme.title: Batwoman
programme.desc: When one of Gotham's former heroes returns to his old stomping ground to settle a score, both Batwoman and Commander Kane find themselves on the defensive

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson guides two more singletons on the dating show based naked attraction alone. First into the studio is 26-year-old Adele from Scotland. After beating cancer, Adele wants to live life to the full and find a guy who feels the same. She is followed by 22-year-old Jack from Manchester. After a dramatic four-stone weight-loss, Jack wants to show off his new body and attract the one for him

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson presents the dating show in which two singletons choose their favourite from a line-up of potential dates who reveal themselves one body part at a time - in all their naked glory. First up is 24-year-old beautician Gemma from West Sussex. She has dated a few love rats and is now searching for her Prince Charming. She is followed by 28-year-old bisexual tattoo artist Jennafer, who is fed up of men and now wants to find the perfect woman. Will any of the contestants be just what Jennafer is looking for?

programme.title: Supernatural
programme.desc: Sam, Dean and Castiel investigate a string of suspicious deaths that have a biblical element to them

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: While the girls go to Disneyland and get made up as princesses, the guys have to promote science as a career option for women at Howard's old middle school. Meanwhile, Raj and Lucy have their first date in a library, where instead of speaking to each other they send text messages

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Bernadette and Howard host a dinner party, but as it falls on Sheldon's Thai food night, he opts to bring his own dinner. As punishment for his lack of manners, the gang decides he should organise Wolowitz's cluttered wardrobe, but he discovers an unopened letter from Howard's father, who hasn't made contact in years

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows. Programmes under consideration include The Haunting of Hill House, Strictly Come Dancing, Killing Eve, Crikey! It's the Irwins, and the news. First aired in November 2018

programme.title: Batwoman
programme.desc: When one of Gotham's former heroes returns to his old stomping ground to settle a score, both Batwoman and Commander Kane find themselves on the defensive

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Chris decides to turn his wedding into the ultimate obstacle course, but his fiancee Charlotte dreams of a wedding day filled with family tradition

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Former boxing champ and groom-to-be Jordan plans a fight-night wedding in front of thousands of people, but his fiancee Danielle dreams of a girly and intimate wedding day

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam abandons Pops to go trick-or-treating with some new friends, but he gets a shock when he discovers what they are really planning. Meanwhile, Beverly dons a costume so she can attend a teenager's Halloween party in secret and protect Barry from social humiliation. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry is thrilled by the freedom his new driving licence brings, until Beverly insists he sticks to her 10 rules of the road at all times. Meanwhile, Pops refuses to wear an emergency alert bracelet and is forced to call Adam when he suffers a fall. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Englishman Andrew and his Sweden-born wife Sophia kick off the competition in Glasgow, with a Swedish-inspired menu. On the second night, Brian and Victoria host with a 'vaguely' Scottish theme, but things don't exactly go as planned when Andrew reveals he doesn't like spice or lentils, both of which are on the menu. Finally, it's David and Sarah's turn to impress with a menu that represents their heritage, with dishes from Russia, Armenia and a Scottish/American dessert

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Beauty queen and bride-to-be Yasmin has her heart set on a fairy-tale wedding. Powerlifter Yiannis is taking Yasmin's dream to the extreme, but will she be the belle of the ball, or will the nuptials have an ugly twist in the tale?

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: During their week apart, Nadia reflects on the pros and cons of her relationship with Anthony. He is reliable and steadfast which makes her feel safe and cared for, but his outspoken nature and stubborn desire to stay in Sydney for the sake of his career has her questioning whether moving for love a second time could end in disaster, as it did in her previous relationship

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel and Joe's friendship with Nate and Gillian is jeopardised when a secret is revealed, and Zander begs Ryder to let him move into the basement without Lennox's knowledge, after he drops out of art school. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Lennox secretly dates her teaching assistant, and when Joe and Mel find out, they try to put an end to the relationship - only things do not go according to plan. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When a fire destroys Boyle's food truck, Pimento unexpectedly shows up at the scene, working as an insurance investigator. Diaz helps Santiago to see that it's fine to get excited about picking her wedding dress, while Gina and Jeffords coach Captain Holt in the art of social media. Comedy, starring Joe Lo Truglio and Jason Mantzoukas

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Boyle organises an elaborate scavenger hunt for Peralta's bachelor party, but the guys take some short cuts that throw the event into disarray. Santiago's bachelorette party goes awry when it comes to light that she once slept with a member of the band that is to play at her wedding reception

programme.title: Young Sheldon

programme.title: Young Sheldon

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tutor Kathy Anderson hosts the third dinner party from Portsmouth and hopes to impress her guests with a Gallic-inspired menu. But all does not go to plan in the kitchen when she experiences a near-disaster with a pineapple, and later in the evening her French entertainment raises quite a few eyebrows

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Stage actor Christopher Marlowe plays host in the fourth dinner party from Portsmouth, and tries to woo his guests with an adventurous menu and theatrical entertainment - including a one-man play involving a drunken waiter, which has a messy outcome

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second night of the competition moves to Moaps Farm in Dane Hill, East Sussex, where Lesley Miller hopes her guests will be charmed by her laid-back attitude. On arrival, the group is wowed by the view, but at breakfast the host's relaxed approach leads to disaster and some of her fellow B&B owners are forced to help out with the service

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From Jabajak in Carmarthenshire, Wales, where sisters Lorraine Lloyd and Amanda Stuart-Robson host in their vineyard and restaurant B&B. The guests are stunned by the surroundings, and businessman Les Woollends and his head chef Lesz Zajac are convinced the sister act have the wow factor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Les Woollends and his head chef Lesz Zajak welcome the contestants to the Clarendon Royal Hotel in Gravesend, Kent. The guests are dazzled on arrival, but Lesley Miller believes the champagne, truffles and fruit bowl are a ploy to win her over. The quality of the food gets good reviews at breakfast, although the guests poke fun at the crockery, and at feedback time criticism comes from an unexpected source and leaves the owner reconsidering his views of his rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants featured throughout the week reunite to revisit the highs and lows of their various stays, and as the B&B owners confront their critics face to face, they discover what the rival parties really thought of the quality of service they encountered. After the deliberations are over, the winning guest house is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Zoe Bailey hosts the first dinner party in Shropshire, and the Zumba instructor is hoping to entertain with a game of gargling while humming a tune. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: LED lighting salesman Richard Mercer hosts the third dinner party in Shropshire. He prepares a main course of paella, before suggesting a game of 'Guess the Gadget' to entertain his guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Youngest contestant of the week Josh Sedgley hosts the fourth dinner party from Shropshire. Some of the guests find his culinary efforts laughable, so he concentrates on the entertainment, which includes semi-naked butlers and a Las Vegas-style casino in his living room

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

However, Paul's pork belly main doesn't live up to one guest's expectations

programme.title: Grand Designs

However, the couple face difficulties as the start of construction proves more a feat of civil engineering than a house build, and work comes to a halt when their self-build mortgage falls through

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Cases involving patients who are under 16, including a baby in cardiac arrest, a boy who fell from a trampoline, and a two-year-old with a suspected anaphylactic reaction

programme.title: Call the Cops
programme.desc: A look at Devon and Cornwall Police's struggle to meet the needs of the communities they serve when reoffending is high, and there are cuts to social and mental health services. Force incident manager Adele faces difficult decisions in protecting the lives of the public as she deals with the most serious and life-threatening cases that come into the Devon and Cornwall police control room. She has to determine whether the use of firearms should be authorised to tackle a 15-year-old in possession of a sword, and if there is time to wait 30 minutes for a negotiator to talk a woman away from the edge of a cliff

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A builder falls at work and dislocates his shoulder in such an unusual way it takes a five-strong team to pull it into place. Alan gets a wake-up call from internet critics, who he believes are trying to shut him down

She has to determine whether the use of firearms should be authorised to tackle a 15-year-old in possession of a sword, and if there is time to wait 30 minutes for a negotiator to talk a woman away from the edge of a cliff

They're constructing three connected pods which can be opened-up or shut-down to meet demand. Charlie has lots of suggestions for how to achieve the agricultural look they're after. The build gets off to a great start, but when money gets tight, Jayne and Alistair are left wondering whether they've made a mistake

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

While at the establishment, Mike and Charlie have a hairy issue, and Jane and Amy encounter a spider, before getting to grips with an ambitious breakfast menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Elvira Barratt welcome the contestants to Barratt's at Ty'n Rhyl, Denbighshire, but the group has an unpleasant experience on arrival. The guests take part in an activity at a local cookery school where they are taught the art of preparing and cooking sheep's testicles. However, come feedback time, the hosts are left feeling distraught

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet to find out who earned the most and have it out with their rivals. Jane and Amy kick off proceedings with a gripe over David and Elvira's coffee criticism, yet are confronted themselves by Zak, who has a score to settle. Mike and Charlie of the Valley Hotel want to get to the bottom of an unwanted intruder during their stay at Wisteria House, and the ladies are challenged again regarding their cleanliness comments. Emotions run high as the cash is counted and the winner is revealed

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Four Sumatran orang-utans escape from their quarters, and tempting them back inside proves easier said than done, while the dominant female meerkat faces trouble from her daughter, who is launching physical attacks upon her mother. A young Asian elephant keeps the rest of the herd entertained as they adapt to the loss of two members to a virus. The keepers believe it needs a playmate its own age, but their hopes rest on the bull elephant mating with the females once more

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Family members rush their chihuahua, Darcy, to the clinic after it is hit by a car. For 18-year-old TaJournée, who is autistic, the dog is a lifeline, and Noel must use pins, nuts and bolts to reconstruct the broken pieces of its crushed pelvis. Meanwhile, the vet finds himself in a tricky situation while performing replacement surgery on a seven-year-old spaniel with hip problems, and a Winchester Prison sniffer dog that is lame in both elbows is brought to the surgery

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge joins Elaine and David as they look around an 1850s chateau with 25 rooms, telling them what to look out for when buying such a vast property. The programme also catches up with Tim and Krys from the first series, who renovated rooms for a boutique B&B in the Dordogne, but have been struggling to juggle parenthood with running the business

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Design engineer Joe Stuart and service designer Lina Nilsson take on the London property market, as they build their own home on a small 38-square-metre plot. Joe designs a split-level house that will cleverly maximise the space, but the project soon runs months behind schedule, and thousands of pounds over their £160,000 budget

programme.title: Inside the Zoo: Down Under
programme.desc: Naomi Watts narrates a documentary that goes behind the scenes at Sydney's Taronga Zoo, which is home to more than 5,000 creatures. In the first edition, keepers must anesthetise and transport an unwell silverback gorilla to the zoo's wildlife hospital

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old Josh is flown in with a possible penile fracture resulting from a canal barge accident, and while his parents make jokes to try to lighten the atmosphere, the injury could have serious repercussions for his future. A man with an injured finger following a night of clubbing contemplates his upcoming gender reassignment therapy, which will include taking hormones, while an 80-year-old with terminal cancer arrives after collapsing at her home

programme.title: The World's Most Extreme
programme.desc: A look at how humans can thrive in harsh terrain, including the inhabitants of a town in the Andes defying sub-zero temperatures and little oxygen to venture deep into gold mines

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson takes on guest captain Johnny Vegas in another round of words-and-numbers chaos. Their team-mates are Alex Brooker and Sara Pascoe, while Claudia Winkleman and Rob Beckett stand in for Rachel Riley on letters-and-numbers duty, and Tom Allen is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. Hosted by Jimmy Carr

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Jayne and Alistair Lewis, a south Lanarkshire couple trying to self-build an innovative, barn-style home which can grow and shrink depending on their needs. They're constructing three connected pods which can be opened-up or shut-down to meet demand. Charlie has lots of suggestions for how to achieve the agricultural look they're after. The build gets off to a great start, but when money gets tight, Jayne and Alistair are left wondering whether they've made a mistake

programme.title: Inside the Zoo: Down Under
programme.desc: Naomi Watts narrates a documentary that goes behind the scenes at Sydney's Taronga Zoo, which is home to more than 5,000 creatures. In the first edition, keepers must anesthetise and transport an unwell silverback gorilla to the zoo's wildlife hospital

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton learns about a potentially life-saving property of discarded shrimp shells and Matt Tebbutt investigates why people cannot seem to get enough of salted caramel. Plus, Jimmy Doherty explores the price differences between fresh and frozen pizzas in supermarkets

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Mauritius to find out what the difference is between white and dark rum, and learns the explosive origins of the term proof

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Having both retired from the Army, Rachel and Steven Grey are hoping to find the perfect property on Costa Rica's Pacific Costa Ballena where they can run a small B&B. Sara Damergi shows them a variety of possible options for their £250,000 budget

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Nantwich and Stoke-on-Trent, where Henry comes across a 1960s moped, and Simon restores an early Mirror Dinghy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores a petrol pump and turns a hubcap into a clock, and Simon O'Brien repurposes a copper immersion heater into a stylish wash basin

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge joins Elaine and David as they look around an 1850s chateau with 25 rooms, telling them what to look out for when buying such a vast property. The programme also catches up with Tim and Krys from the first series, who renovated rooms for a boutique B&B in the Dordogne, but have been struggling to juggle parenthood with running the business

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to North Somerset, where he helps Antony and Hayley Wild build a contemporary four-bedroom home overlooking the Severn estuary. Having waited 26 years to finally build their own house, maths teacher Hayley and professional project manager Antony are aiming high. However, the budget cannot keep pace with their ambitions

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Who'd a Thought It in Grafty Green, Kent, where partners Joe Mallett and Alex Rea Baldwin hope to impress their fellow guests. On arrival, Liz and Brian are excited over their two hot tubs, and Jackie and Paul are more than happy with the complimentary champagne. However, Matthew and Liam are underwhelmed by the cleanliness levels

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the New Osborne Hotel in Blackpool on their second night, where proprietor Matthew Bracegirdle and entertainment manager Liam hope their guests feel at home in their family friendly establishment. However, Jackie and Paul are unimpressed by their small room, and Joe and Alex find the cleanliness levels lacking

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Fairy Falls Hotel in Trefriw, Conwy County Borough, where B&B newcomers Jackie and Paul Langshaw hope to impress the other contestants. They take their guests to a local maze in the afternoon and, after an uncomfortable night's sleep for Alex, some of the breakfast orders are misplaced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Husband and wife Liz and Brian Jardine welcome their guests to The Great Trewern B&B, Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where they enjoy the quiet life. Joe and Alex feel far away from it all, but the shared bathroom facilities fail to impress the other guests. At dinner, talk turns to first impressions and it appears it is all water under the bridge for Jackie and Joe, but she remains upset with the feedback Matthew and Liam gave her

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet one final time to find out what they have been paid. Joe Mallett and Alex Rea Baldwin kick things off by confronting Matthew Bracegirdle and Liam Halewood about their comments regarding the cleanliness of their establishment, and Jackie and Paul Langshaw complain about their poor breakfast score

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pedro the giant anteater is about to be reunited with long-term mate Bliss, but falls ill and faces a major operation, with an uncertain outcome. Chester Zoo's extremely rare bald ibises produce a batch of new chicks, which are destined to be released back into the wild in southern Spain. There is also a chance to witness the reunion of rhino couple Beni and Asha, who have been kept separate for four years

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A one-year-old St Bernard suffering with hip dysplasia is brought to the clinic by the Warden family. Having recently lost the the dog's brother to a similar illness, emotions are running high. Meanwhile, Noel treats a 13-year-old collie that has been hit by a car, and tries to devise a new bionic paw for a cat with a badly deformed hind foot

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim battles to get a fence built for their marauding flock of 25 sheep, and Krys battles to balance the books. They race to get their chateau ready for their first paying guests as Lee struggles with a chandelier. Elaine and David continue the search for their dream property, visiting a 17th-century ex-priory in Normandy and taking on board all that Dick has taught them

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Following the work of Ed and Rowena Waghorn as they continue to build a handcrafted, five-bedroom house on an eight acre smallholding in Herefordshire, 10 years in the making. Progress on the build was slow as Ed decided to do most of the work himself, and after four years the house was still a shell. In 2012 Kevin McCloud decided to follow the build for another five years. In the end, it's just possible this house might have turned out to be a masterpiece

programme.title: Japan's Tsunami: How It Happened
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the scientific factors behind the magnitude nine earthquake and the resultant tsunami that struck Japan on March 11, 2011. Professor of geological sciences Roger Bilham views the effects of the devastation from the air, and journalist Callum Macrae travels to the north of the country, where survivors in the fishing villages of Sendai and Ofunato are struggling to cope in the aftermath

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Cases involving patients who are under 16, including a baby in cardiac arrest, a boy who fell from a trampoline, and a two-year-old with a suspected anaphylactic reaction

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson and Sean Lock face off once again in another unpredictable battle of words and numbers, with help from Kevin Bridges and Rebecca Front. Rachel Riley is on maths-and-letters duty, and Joe Lycett is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The air ambulance teams are called out to help a man who is having a heart attack, and there is a dramatic night-time car crash in Norfolk

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to North Somerset, where he helps Antony and Hayley Wild build a contemporary four-bedroom home overlooking the Severn estuary. Having waited 26 years to finally build their own house, maths teacher Hayley and professional project manager Antony are aiming high. However, the budget cannot keep pace with their ambitions

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay visits family-run Luigi's in Orange County, California, where lazy shortcuts following the retirement of the owner's father have led the business to suffer. Ramsay faces the daunting task of reuniting a feuding family, before turning around the struggling restaurant, which currently has 126 dishes on the menu

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton uncovers something surprising about small hen's eggs, Matt Tebbutt tracks down where the bubbles in beer come from, and Jimmy Doherty investigates why supermarket burgers should not be eaten when the meat is still pink

programme.title: Last Stop Garage
programme.desc: A client needs improvements on his SUV to get him to his off-the-grid cabin. Apart from arriving in one piece, his biggest concern is running out of gas to power his generator

programme.title: Last Stop Garage

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik, coastal capital of Iceland. His aim is to enjoy the perfect winter break, enriched with a smorgasbord featuring the best ingredients the country has to offer - fjord-reared roast lamb, rye bread baked in volcanic sand and langoustine soup. Rick also resumes his search for the perfect cod

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Coverage of the offbeat event which comes from the Cauberg, a hill in the southern town of Valkenburg, Holland

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May pay tribute to Saab, the Swedish car manufacturer that created several successful models since the late 1940s, but has now ceased production. Richard Hammond takes the passenger's seat in a Skoda rally car that races a rocket-propelled flying man, and actor Matt Smith gets behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car. Jeremy also shreds the tyres of the noisy Maserati GranTurismo MC Stradale and the 510bhp Mercedes-Benz C63 AMG Black Series

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May test the KTM X-Bow, the Morgan Three Wheeler and the Caterham R500 at Donington Park racing circuit. Jeremy also creates his own version of the Battle of Britain using an aircraft-engined Bentley and a flame-spitting BMW

programme.title: American Pickers: Best Of
programme.desc: Mike and Frank learn it is not always easy getting what they want when they face down some of the toughest negotiators on the road

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: Mexican Cartels exploit an old migrant route, thick with brush, to traffic drugs across the Rio Grande. Plus, in Nogales Arizona, law enforcement works round the clock to counter traffickers' endlessly inventive techniques for smuggling drugs. Custom and Border Protection officers take apart one particularly suspicious vehicle that may be hiding drugs in a compartment that's extremely difficult to access

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: Custom and Border Protection officers in the Port of Miami and on the legendary Miami River are on high alert over a resurgence of cocaine smuggling

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees UK bidders battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages - with the opening episode featuring a bidding war over a mystery object, and the gang competing for a lot of occult objects and a coffin

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Dan gambles on a lot he might later regret in when the gang visits Milton Keynes, while Nat lets her heart rule her head on a sale

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: The fortune-finding show continues in Bentwaters, with the contents of some old army bunkers exciting the bidders, while a vintage box captures Rupert's attention

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: The chance of winning a car at a blind auction in Perth sends the gang into a bidding frenzy. Presented by Sean Kelly

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian tests his preconceptions of Albania, guided on a whistlestop tour of the country by local TV presenter and journalist Erjona Rusi. Along the way, he tries to discover why Albania has remained a stubbornly unfashionable holiday destination, meeting those at the extremes of the country, from the rural farmers to the billionaire playboys who live in the cities in the company of supermodels

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.title: Live at the Apollo

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a culinary journey by sea and land to explore the Far East's most diverse food cultures. He begins in Cambodia, where tourists are beginning to return after the fall of the Khmer Rouge regime. As well as discovering the joys of freshly caught swimmer crabs with green peppercorns, he learns how to cook the country's most famous dish - fish amok - and samples lok lak, made with marinated beef. He also visits the Angkor Wat temple and joins a rice barge to travel down the Mekong River

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mail's delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May each buy a rear-wheel-drive coupe for £1,500 and find themselves entered into a French ice race. Richard discovers a way to fill the waiting time for flights by inventing the sport of airport-vehicle racing, Jeremy gets behind the wheel of the BMW Z4, and actress Sienna Miller is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Quiz in which four famous faces go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week, with BBC Breakfast's Naga Munchetty, singer and actor Jordan Stephens, presenter and actress Sally Lindsay and comedian David O'Doherty kicking thing off. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the weekly scores are tallied resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Offbeat tourism show in which Richard Ayoade is joined by celebrity guests to enjoy the most efficient weekend break possible. He begins in Barcelona with comedienne and actress Kathy Burke, experiencing the best of this popular holiday destination in just 48 hours. The pair sample adventurous cuisine, including potatoes coated in edible clay, and a cava tasting session quickly descends into disarray. Other highlights include a visit to the Barcelona Football Club museum, a guided tricycle tour, and a trip to see the surrealist work of Catalan artist Joan Miro

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by fellow comedian Adam Hills on a whirlwind weekend in Turkey's largest city. He wants to show The Last Leg host how to holiday with ruthless efficiency, as they cross the River Bosphorus from Europe to Asia, comparing a private yacht tour with the low-price public ferry. The pair have an authentic Turkish shave at a local barbershop that turns out to be a lot closer than they were expecting, and share an awkward moment at a local bath and massage parlour. There's also a visit to the Grand Bazaar market, where both men have a go at haggling

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Gyles Brandreth, Victoria Coren Mitchell and Jimmy Carr join series regular Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme of 'not nearly' in an extended edition of the comedy panel show. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian is in the Canadian Arctic for this festive special of the travelogue in which he explores countries rarely visited by tourists. He wants to know whether there is more to this area than the prospect of freezing to death or being eaten by a polar bear. Travelling across frozen wastelands with host Johnny Issaluk, he spends a week in the largest territory, getting to grips with the Inuit hunter's way of life - not an easy task for vegan Romesh. He also witnesses the erosion and resurgence of Inuit traditions and discover why alcohol is often restricted in the communities
