programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: A woman redevelops her flat in west London, striving to achieve a high level of finish with a modest budget in the hope of making it more appealing to potential tenants. Sarah Beeny presents

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: A couple from Lincoln make the risky decision to leave their jobs so they can concentrate on renovating an old dairy

programme.title: Exploits at West Poley (1985)
programme.desc: Two boys exploring an underground cave divert the course of a river, with surprising repercussions for two towns. Children's period drama, with Brenda Fricker and Charlie Condou

programme.title: The Battle of Billy's Pond (1976)
programme.desc: A boy investigates after his cat becomes ill after eating a dead fish from a local pond. He discovers the fish was killed by industrial waste that a company has been dumping in the water - but the owners will go to any lengths to keep their misdeeds a secret. Family drama, starring Ben Buckton and Andrew Ashby

programme.title: Calculated Risk (1963)
programme.desc: An ex-convict plans one last daring heist, by tunneling into a bank vault through the basement of a neighbouring house. Crime drama, starring William Lucas and John Rutland

programme.title: The Case of the Frightened Lady (1940)
programme.desc: A woman employed as a secretary to her aristocratic aunt has to contend with sinister servants plotting behind her back and a scheme by her employer to marry her off to her own cousin. The situation is complicated further when the family's doctor is accused of murder. Thriller, starring Marius Goring and Penelope Dudley-Ward

programme.title: Date with a Dream (1948)
programme.desc: Four wartime performers well known for their rousing concert recitals decide to reform and become a successful nightclub act all over again. Upbeat comedy, starring Wally Patch, Len Lowe, Terry-Thomas, Norman Wisdom and Jean Carson

programme.title: In the Doghouse (1961)
programme.desc: A newly qualified vet struggles to compete against an unscrupulous rival. Comedy, starring Leslie Phillips, Peggy Cummins, Hattie Jacques and James Booth

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks along the Isle of Man's stunning coast and visits Sprotbrough in South Yorkshire

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fay Davies advises Dee Mullinger, who is looking for a home in the central area of Portugal's picturesque Algarve. The single mother hopes to secure a four-bedroom villa in a safe location with easy access to an English-speaking school for around £200,000

programme.title: Great SAS Missions
programme.desc: Accounts of daring initiatives by the elite British unit, beginning with a 1941 operation in North Africa in which a commando set up a special unit to create trouble for Rommel's army behind enemy lines. Interviews with former SAS soldiers and cutting-edge graphics are used to tell the story of the raid

programme.title: Great SAS Missions
programme.desc: Using interviews, computer graphics and reconstructions to recount the Second World War SAS mission to infiltrate enemy borders. Heavily armoured cars managed to destroy a number of Hitler's supply lines, and by the time the fighting in North Africa had ended the unit had grown into two full regiments

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen seeks a boyfriend for her socially inept elder sister Laura, so invites nervous horticulturalist Mike to a party. The pair discover they share a dislike of being fixed up, so depart together to prevent Helen objecting to them leaving. Bob Larbey's comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen and Phil are keen to know how things went between unlikely lovebirds Laura and Mike, so both end up invited to lunch - where they relate conflicting stories. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: A Song for Jenny
programme.desc: Drama, based on Julie Nicholson's book of the same name, which explores her response to her daughter's death at Edgware Road tube station in the July 7 bombings. The rollercoaster of emotions experienced by Julie and her family after the attack is explored; from hope, denial and acceptance, to grief and the desire to honour Jenny. The programme coincides with the forthcoming 10th anniversary of the 7/7 bombings in London and is a tribute to those who lost their lives in the attack

programme.title: We Still Steal the Old Way (2017)
programme.desc: After their exploits in We Still Kill the Old Way, criminal outfit the Archer Gang encounters an old enemy when a daring heist ends with them behind bars, however that doesn't stop them attempting to break out an old friend. Prison drama, starring Ian Ogilvy, Billy Murray, Julian Glover and Lysette Anthony

programme.title: Hooligan Escape: The Russian Job (2018)
programme.desc: Five British football fans are arrested while attending the World Cup in Russia. However, their problems get even worse when a corrupt guard hands them over to an old enemy who has been plotting revenge for years. Thriller, starring Ali Bastian and Charlie Wernham

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist is joined by fellow musicians for an impromptu jam session, as well as a chat about their lives in rock. In this edition he is joined by Alice Cooper

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The travellers cross the border into Germany, but trouble brews as some of them have problems waking up and stomaching the local food

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The passengers reach the German spa town of Baden Baden, where they indulge in a spot of relaxation and learn to make Black Forest gateaux. Meanwhile, a fairy tale causes problems for tour leader Brendan

programme.title: Bloomfield (1970)
programme.desc: An Israeli footballer nearing the end of his career is tempted to accept a bribe to throw his final game. A devoted young fan and a woman who is in love with him prick his conscience, but even if he chooses to do the right thing, his failing skills may not be enough to triumph over younger, fitter opposition. Sporting drama based on Joseph Gross' novel, co-directed by and starring Richard Harris, with Romy Schneider and Kim Burfield

programme.title: Piccadilly Incident (1946)
programme.desc: A Wren is pronounced missing in action, but returns three years later to find her husband has remarried. Melodrama, starring Anna Neagle and Michael Wilding

programme.title: Spitfire Women
programme.desc: The story of the 168 female pilots who served in Britain's Air Transport Auxiliary - an organisation responsible for delivering aircraft to the RAF on the front lines of battle during the Second World War. Despite having no radio or navigation equipment, ATA pilots were expected to fly any kind of aircraft to the destinations where they were most needed, often risking their lives in the process. The film also features interviews with the last surviving female veterans, who speak about the dangers they encountered in the skies and the discrimination they faced back home

programme.title: I Train My Hands for War

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between from around Britain. Andrew White walks on a disused railway in the Peak District and alongside the Caledonian Canal

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra

programme.title: In the Doghouse (1961)
programme.desc: A newly qualified vet struggles to compete against an unscrupulous rival. Comedy, starring Leslie Phillips, Peggy Cummins, Hattie Jacques and James Booth

programme.title: The October Man (1947)
programme.desc: A chemist with suicidal tendencies is falsely accused of murder - and his precarious mental state is further upset by taunts from the real killer. Psychological thriller, adapted from a novel by Eric Ambler, starring John Mills, Joan Greenwood and Joyce Carey

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fay Davies advises opera-lover Rosie Wilson and her daughter on purchasing a second home in Tuscany with wonderful views of the countryside on a budget of £300,000

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Homeowners work to increase the value of their properties, redesigning and refurbishing for maximum profits

programme.title: Spitfire Women
programme.desc: The story of the 168 female pilots who served in Britain's Air Transport Auxiliary - an organisation responsible for delivering aircraft to the RAF on the front lines of battle during the Second World War. Despite having no radio or navigation equipment, ATA pilots were expected to fly any kind of aircraft to the destinations where they were most needed, often risking their lives in the process. The film also features interviews with the last surviving female veterans, who speak about the dangers they encountered in the skies and the discrimination they faced back home

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and the team are in Chester to learn about an experiment to find out whether standing up a few more hours each day could have major health benefits. Gabriel Weston witnesses a womb transplant, Saleyha Ahsan shows how to stop someone choking and Chris van Tulleken finds out if vitamin supplements are important to wellbeing

programme.title: City of Two Springs
programme.desc: Documentary about the international effort to rebuild the city of Mosul, seen through the eyes of a local child and a senior UN figure

programme.title: 198 Grand (2019)
programme.desc: Three hapless criminals from South London get mixed up in an underworld double-cross, and find themselves in possession of a large sum of money that puts their lives in danger. Crime drama, starring Andy Frith and Dean Kilbey

programme.title: Hooligan Escape: The Russian Job (2018)
programme.desc: Five British football fans are arrested while attending the World Cup in Russia. However, their problems get even worse when a corrupt guard hands them over to an old enemy who has been plotting revenge for years. Thriller, starring Ali Bastian and Charlie Wernham

programme.title: Bright Young Things (2003)
programme.desc: A would-be writer tries to raise enough money to marry his sweetheart, but a lost manuscript and several unlucky bets put his goal out of reach. Desperate to turn matters round, he launches himself into 1930s high society, hoping to curry favour with the rich and influential. Comedy based on Evelyn Waugh's Vile Bodies, directed by Stephen Fry and starring Emily Mortimer, Stephen Campbell Moore, James McAvoy and Michael Sheen

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts a celebrity version of the classic game show, in which he invites well-known faces and their clans to win £30,000 for charity. Radio 1 DJ Chris Moyles plays for the Robert Ogden Centre and the Iain Rennie Hospice at Home, while presenter Fearne Cotton aims to win big for Macmillan Cancer Support

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts another celebrity edition of the classic game show, in which he invites well-known faces to team up with their relatives in a bid to win a prize of up to £30,000 for charity. Actor Nicola Stapleton joins her clan to play for Cancer Research, while comedian Johnny Vegas and his family aim to win big for the Willowbrook Hospice

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: Adventure game show in which 20 men and women travel to Argentina to compete in physically demanding challenges on a purpose-built obstacle course. Richard Hammond presents from the safety of the studio, while Amanda Byram meets the contestants on the course itself
